Name: university_mechanics_Mechanics_1_University
Header:

import PHYSlib.Foundations.SIDeriv

unseal Rat.add Rat.mul Rat.sub Rat.inv
variable (m:Mass) (v:Speed) (F:Force) (p:Momentum) (E:Energy) (h:Length) (x:Displacement) (t:Time) (a:Acceleration)
Theorem:
theorem Mechanics_1_University
  (hd: x = -5150 • kilo meter)
  (ht: t = 14 • day)
  (h_v: v = x / t):
  v = (-5150000/1209600 : ℚ) • meter / second := by sorry

simp [←Scalar.val_inj, hd, ht, h_v, kilo, meter, day, hour, minute, second]
  field_simp
  norm_num
Informal_statement: Question 1 In an experiment, a shearwater (a seabird) was taken from itsnest, flown 5150 km away, and released. The bird found its way back to its nest 14 days after release. lf we place the origin at the nest and extend the +x-axis to the release point, what was the bird's average velocity in m/s for the return flight

Name: university_mechanics_Mechanics_3_University
Header:
import Mathlib
import Aesop
import Mathlib.Tactic
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SI
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SIDeriv
import PHYSlib.Mechanics.Displacement
import PHYSlib.Mechanics.Motion
import PHYSlib.Thermodynamics.Volume
open BigOperators Real Nat Topology Rat UnitsSystem SI SI.Mechanics SI.Thermodynamics
unseal Rat.add Rat.mul Rat.sub Rat.inv
variable (m:Mass) (v:Speed) (F:Force) (p:Momentum) (E:Energy) (h:Length) (x:Displacement) (t:Time) (a:Acceleration)
Theorem:
theorem Mechanics_3_University
  (x_0 x_1 : Length)(t_0 t_1 dt t : Time)(v_0 v_1 : Speed)(a: Acceleration)
  (xf xf1: Time → Length)(vf : Time → Speed)
  (ht0 : t_0 = 0 • second)
  (ht1 : t_1 = 4 • second)
  (ht : dt = t_1 - t_0)
  (ha : a = (v_1 -v_0)/dt)
  (hv : ∀ t, vf t = v_0 + a * t/1)
  (hv1: v_1 = vf dt)
  (hx: ∀ t, xf t = (a * t**2)/2 + v_0 * t)
  (hxx: ∀ t, xf1 t = (3 • meter / second**2)* t**2 - 2 • meter / second * t)
  (hxxx: xf = xf1):
  (a = 6 • meter / second**2 ∧ v_0 = -2 • meter / second) := by sorry
Statement:

variable (m:Mass) (v:Speed) (F:Force) (p:Momentum) (E:Energy) (h:Length) (x:Displacement) (t:Time) (a:Acceleration)
theorem Mechanics_3_University
  (x_0 x_1 : Length)(t_0 t_1 dt t : Time)(v_0 v_1 : Speed)(a: Acceleration)
  (xf xf1: Time → Length)(vf : Time → Speed)
  (ht0 : t_0 = 0 • second)
  (ht1 : t_1 = 4 • second)
  (ht : dt = t_1 - t_0)
  (ha : a = (v_1 -v_0)/dt)
  (hv : ∀ t, vf t = v_0 + a * t/1)
  (hv1: v_1 = vf dt)
  (hx: ∀ t, xf t = (a * t**2)/2 + v_0 * t)
  (hxx: ∀ t, xf1 t = (3 • meter / second**2)* t**2 - 2 • meter / second * t)
  (hxxx: xf = xf1):
  (a = 6 • meter / second**2 ∧ v_0 = -2 • meter / second) := by sorry
Informal_statement: Question 3 Suppose an object is moving along a straight line, find its instantaneous velocity as a function of time during the time period 0 ≤ 𝑡 ≤ 4, if its position 𝑥(𝑡) during the same period is given by: 𝑥(𝑡) = 3𝑡^2 − 2𝑡

Name: university_mechanics_Mechanics_11_University
Header:
import Mathlib
import Aesop
import Mathlib.Tactic
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SI
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SIDeriv
import PHYSlib.Mechanics.Displacement
import PHYSlib.Mechanics.Motion
import PHYSlib.Thermodynamics.Volume
open BigOperators Real Nat Topology Rat UnitsSystem SI SI.Mechanics SI.Thermodynamics
unseal Rat.add Rat.mul Rat.sub Rat.inv
variable (m:Mass) (v:Speed) (F:Force) (p:Momentum) (E:Energy) (h:Length) (x:Displacement) (t:Time) (a:Acceleration)
Theorem:
theorem Mechanics_11_University
  (A B C Ax Ay Bx By Cx Cy Rx Ry R : Length)(θA θB θC θR: PhysAngle)
  (hA: A = (72.4:ℚ) • meter)
  (hB: B = (57.3:ℚ) • meter)
  (hC: C = (17.8:ℚ) • meter)
  (hθA: θA = (58 * π /180) • radian)
  (hθB: θB = (216 * π /180) • radian)
  (hθC: θC = (270 * π /180) • radian)
  (hAx: Ax = cos (Scalar.val θA) • A )
  (hAy: Ay = sin (Scalar.val θA) • A )
  (hBx: Bx = cos (Scalar.val θB) • B )
  (hBy: By = sin (Scalar.val θB) • B )
  (hCx: Cx = cos (Scalar.val θC) • C )
  (hCy: Cy = sin (Scalar.val θC) • C )
  (hRx: Rx = Ax + Bx + Cx)
  (hRy: Ry = Ay + By + Cy)
  (hd: R**2 = Rx**2 + Ry**2)
  (hθR: tan (Scalar.val θR) =  (Scalar.val Ry / Scalar.val Rx : ℝ)):
  (Rx = (-7.99 : ℚ) • meter ∧ Ry = (9.92 : ℚ) • meter ∧ R = (12.7 : ℚ) • meter ∧ θR = (129 * π /180) • radian ) := by sorry
Statement:
import Mathlib
import Aesop
import Mathlib.Tactic
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SI
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SIDeriv
import PHYSlib.Mechanics.Displacement
import PHYSlib.Mechanics.Motion
import PHYSlib.Thermodynamics.Volume
open BigOperators Real Nat Topology Rat UnitsSystem SI SI.Mechanics SI.Thermodynamics
unseal Rat.add Rat.mul Rat.sub Rat.inv
variable (m:Mass) (v:Speed) (F:Force) (p:Momentum) (E:Energy) (h:Length) (x:Displacement) (t:Time) (a:Acceleration)
theorem Mechanics_11_University
  (A B C Ax Ay Bx By Cx Cy Rx Ry R : Length)(θA θB θC θR: PhysAngle)
  (hA: A = (72.4:ℚ) • meter)
  (hB: B = (57.3:ℚ) • meter)
  (hC: C = (17.8:ℚ) • meter)
  (hθA: θA = (58 * π /180) • radian)
  (hθB: θB = (216 * π /180) • radian)
  (hθC: θC = (270 * π /180) • radian)
  (hAx: Ax = cos (Scalar.val θA) • A )
  (hAy: Ay = sin (Scalar.val θA) • A )
  (hBx: Bx = cos (Scalar.val θB) • B )
  (hBy: By = sin (Scalar.val θB) • B )
  (hCx: Cx = cos (Scalar.val θC) • C )
  (hCy: Cy = sin (Scalar.val θC) • C )
  (hRx: Rx = Ax + Bx + Cx)
  (hRy: Ry = Ay + By + Cy)
  (hd: R**2 = Rx**2 + Ry**2)
  (hθR: tan (Scalar.val θR) =  (Scalar.val Ry / Scalar.val Rx : ℝ)):
  (Rx = (-7.99 : ℚ) • meter ∧ Ry = (9.92 : ℚ) • meter ∧ R = (12.7 : ℚ) • meter ∧ θR = (129 * π /180) • radian ) := by sorry
Informal_statement: Question 11 Three players on a reality TV show are brought to the center of a large, flat field. Each is given a meter stick, a compass, a calculator, ashovel, and (in a different order for each contestant) the following three displacements: A: 72.4 m, 32.0° east of north B: 57.3 m, 36.0° south of west C: 17.8 m due south The three displacements lead to the point in the field where the keys to a new Porsche are buried. Two players start measuring immediately, but the winner first calculates where to go. What does she calculate? Answer: A_x=Acos0=(72.4m)(cos58.0°)= 38.37 m , A_y=Asin0:=(72.4m)(sin58.0°)=61.40 m Distance Angle   x-component y-component A = 72.4 m  58.0°    38.37m   61.34m B =57.3 m   216.0°   -46.36m  -33.68m C = 17.8m   270.0°    -0.00m   -17.80m R_x= -7.99 R_y= 9.92 m R = 12.7 m The correct value is 180°+(-51°)= 129°or 39° west of north.

  (hdt3 : dt_3 = (0.001: ℚ)• second)

During the first 2.0 s of the chasethe cheetah's x-coordinate varies with time according to the equation x = 20 m+(5.0 m/s^2)t^2, (c) Find its instantaneous velocity at t_1= 1.0 s by taking delta_t = 0.1 s, then 0.01 s, then 0.001 s. Answer: delta_t = 0.1s x = 26.05m va_x = (26.05m - 25m) / (1.1s - 1.0s) = 10.5 m/s delta_t = 0.01s va_x = 10.05 m/s delta_t = 0.001s va_x = 10.005 m/s

Informal_statement: Question 20 An astronaut has left an orbiting spacecraft to test a new personal maneuvering unit. As she moves along a straight line, her partner on the spacecraft measures her velocity every 2.0 s, starting at time t = 1.0 s: t     vx      t       vx 1.0 s 0.8 m/s 9.0 s  -0.4 m/s 3.0 s 1.2 m/s 11.0 s -1.0 m/s 5.0 s 1.6 m/s 13.0 s -1.6 m/s 7.0 s 1.2 m/s 15.0 s -0.8 m/s Find the average x-acceleration, and state whether the speed of the as- tronaut increases or decreases over each of these 2.0 s time intervals: (d) t1 = 13.0 s to t2 = 15.0 s.

Informal_statement: Question 21 Suppose the x-velocity v_x, of the car at any time t is given by the equation v_x=60 m/s +(0.50 m/s^3 )t^2 (a) Find the change in x-velocity of the car in the time interval t_1 = 1.0s t_2 = 3.0 s. Answer: v_1 = 60 m/s + (0.50 m/s^3)(1.0 s)^2 = 60.5 m/s v_2 = 60 m/s + (0.50 m/s^3)(3.0 s)^2 = 64.5 m/s delta_v_x = v_2 - v_1 = 64.5 m/s - 60.5 m/s = 4.0 m/s

  (hv_o : ∀ t, vf_o t = v_o + a_o * t/1)
  (hv1_o: v_1 = vf_o t_1)
  (hx1_o: x_1 = xf_o t_1):
  (t = (10 : ℚ) • second ∧ v_1 = (30 : ℚ) • meter / second ∧ x_m = (150 : ℚ) • meter ∧ x_o = (150 : ℚ) • meter) := by sorry
Informal_statement: QUESTION 27 A motorist traveling at a constant 15 m/s(54 km/h) passes a schoolcrossing where the speed limit is 10 m/s (36 km/h). Just as the motoristpasses the school-crossing sign, a police officer on a motorcycle stoppedthere starts in pursuit with constant acceleration 3.0 m/s^2. (a) How much time elapses before the officer passes the motorist? At that time, (b) what is the officer's speed and (c) how far has each vehicletraveled?

  (t_0 t_1 dt t : Time)(v_x0 v_x1 dv_x v_y0 v_y1 dv_y : Speed)(aa_x aa_y : Acceleration)

  (ht0 : t_0 = 0 • second)

  (ht : dt = t_1 - t_0)
  (hxx: ∀ t, vfx t = (- (0.5 : ℚ) • meter / second**2) * t)
  (hyy: ∀ t, vfy t = 1 • meter / second + ((0.075 : ℚ) • meter / second**3)* t**2)

  (x y : Length)(t : Time)(α_0 α_1: PhysAngle)
  (v_x v_y v v_0: Speed)

  (vxt vyt: Time → Speed)
  (ht : t = (2.00: ℚ) • second)
  (hv_0 : v_0 = (37.0:ℚ) • meter / second)
  (ha_0 : α_0 = ((53.1:ℚ) * π / 180) • radian)
  (hvx: ∀ t, vxt t = cos (Scalar.val α_0) • v_0)
  (hx: v_x = vxt t)
  (hvy: ∀ t, vyt t = sin (Scalar.val α_0) • v_0 - g * t / 1)
  (hy: v_y = vyt t)
  (hv: v**2 = v_x**2 + v_y**2)
  (hxx: ∀ t, xt t = (cos (Scalar.val α_0) • v_0) * t)
  (hx1: x = xt t)
  (hyy: ∀ t, yt t = (sin (Scalar.val α_0) • v_0) * t - (g * t**2)/2)
  (hy1: y = yt t)
  (hα1: tan (Scalar.val α_1) = ((Scalar.val v_y) / (Scalar.val v_x)) ):
  (x = (44.4 : ℚ) • meter ∧ y = (39.6 : ℚ) • meter ∧
   v_x = (22.2 : ℚ) • meter / second ∧ v_y = (10.0 : ℚ) • meter / second ∧
   v = (24.4 : ℚ) • meter / second) := by sorry

  (h : Length)(α_0: PhysAngle)(t t_1 : Time)
  (v_y v_0y v_0: Speed)
  (vyt: Time → Speed)
  (hv_0 : v_0 = (37.0:ℚ) • meter / second)
  (ha_0 : α_0 = ((53.1:ℚ) * π / 180) • radian)
  (hv_0y : v_0y = sin (Scalar.val α_0) • v_0)
  (hvy: ∀ t, vyt t = sin (Scalar.val α_0) • v_0 - g * t / 1)
  (hv_y: v_y = vyt t_1)
  (hv_y: v_y = 0 • meter / second)
  (hh: h = v_0y * t_1 - (g * t_1**2)/2):

  (v_y v_0y v_0: Speed)
  (vyt: Time → Speed)
  (hv_0 : v_0 = (37.0:ℚ) • meter / second)
  (ha_0 : α_0 = ((53.1:ℚ) * π / 180) • radian)
  (hv_0y : v_0y = sin (Scalar.val α_0) • v_0)
  (hvy: ∀ t, vyt t = sin (Scalar.val α_0) • v_0 - g * t / 1)
  (hv_y: v_y = vyt t_1)
  (hv_y: v_y = 0 • meter / second)
  (hh: h = v_0y * t_1 - (g * t_1**2)/2):
  (t_1 = (3.02 : ℚ) • second ∧ h = (44.7 : ℚ) • meter) := by sorry
Informal_statement: Question 48 A batter hits a baseball so that it leaves the bat at speed $v_0 = 37.0~\mathrm{m/s}$ at an angle $\alpha_0 = 53.1^\circ$. Find the time when the ball reaches the highest point of its flight, and its height $h$ at this time. Answer: v_y = v_0y - g*t_1 t_1 = v_0y/g = 29.6m/s / 9.8m/s^2 = 3.02s h = 29.6m/s * 3.02s - 1/2 * 9.8m/s^2 * (3.02s)^2 = 44.7m

  (hv_0y : v_0y = sin (Scalar.val α_0) • v_0)

  (hvy: ∀ t, vyt t = sin (Scalar.val α_0) • v_0 - g * t / 1)

Informal_statement: Question 49 A batter hits a baseball so that it leaves the bat at speed $v_0 = 37.0~\mathrm{m/s}$ at an angle $\alpha_0 = 53.1^\circ$. Find the \textit{horizontal range} $R$—that is, the horizontal distance from the starting point to where the ball hits the ground—and the ball’s velocity just before it hits. Answer: Given y = 0 = v_0y * t_2 - (g * t_2**2)/2 Have t_2 = 0s or t_2 = 6.04s, take t_2 = 6.04s R = v_0x * t_2 = 22.2m/s * 6.04s = 134m v_y = v_0y - g*t_2 = 29.6m/s - 9.8m/s^2 * 6.04s = -29.6m/s

  (x y : Length)(α_0: PhysAngle)(t t_1 : Time)

  (vxt vyt: Time → Speed)

  (ht : t = (0.98: ℚ) • second)
  (hv_0 : v_0 = (10.0:ℚ) • meter / second)
  (ha_0 : α_0 = - ((20.0:ℚ) * π / 180) • radian)
  (hv_0x : v_0x = cos (Scalar.val α_0) • v_0)
  (hv_0y : v_0y = sin (Scalar.val α_0) • v_0)
  (hvx: ∀ t, vxt t = cos (Scalar.val α_0) • v_0)
  (hvy: ∀ t, vyt t = sin (Scalar.val α_0) • v_0 - g * t / 1)
  (hv_x: v_x = vxt t)
  (hv_y: v_y = vyt t)
  (hv_yz: v_y = 0 • meter / second)
  (hh: 0 • meter = - y + v_0y * t/1 - (g * t**2)/2)

  (x = (9.2 : ℚ) • meter) := by sorry
Informal_statement: Question 51 You throw a ball from your window $8.0~\mathrm{m}$ above the ground. When the ball leaves your hand, it is moving at $10.0~\mathrm{m/s}$ at an angle of $20.0^\circ$ below the horizontal. How far horizontally from your window will the ball hit the ground? Ignore air resistance. Answer: 1/2gt^2-(v_0 sinα_0)t + y = 0 t = -1.7s or 0.98s We discard the negative root, since it refers to a time before the ball left your hand. x = (v_0 cosα_0)t = (10.0 m/s cos 20.0°)(0.98 s) = 9.2 m

If the car is traveling at a constant $40~\mathrm{m/s}$ (about $89~\mathrm{mi/h}$, or $144~\mathrm{km/h}$) on level ground, what is the radius $R$ of the tightest unbanked curve it can negotiate?

  (F n w : Force)(m : Mass)(a_x a_y : Acceleration)
  (hF : F = 20 • newton)
  (hm : m = 40 • kilogram)
  (ha_x : a_x = F / m)
  (ha_y : a_y = 0 • meter / second**2):

Statement:
import Mathlib
import Aesop
import Mathlib.Tactic
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SI
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SIDeriv
import PHYSlib.Mechanics.Displacement
import PHYSlib.Mechanics.Motion
import PHYSlib.Thermodynamics.Volume
open BigOperators Real Nat Topology Rat UnitsSystem SI SI.Mechanics SI.Thermodynamics
unseal Rat.add Rat.mul Rat.sub Rat.inv
variable (m:Mass) (v:Speed) (F:Force) (p:Momentum) (E:Energy) (h:Length) (x:Displacement) (t:Time) (a:Acceleration)
theorem Mechanics_59_University
  (F n w : Force)(m : Mass)(a_x a_y : Acceleration)
  (hF : F = 20 • newton)
  (hm : m = 40 • kilogram)
  (ha_x : a_x = F / m)
  (ha_y : a_y = 0 • meter / second**2):
  (a_x = (0.5 : ℚ) • meter / second**2 ∧ a_y = 0 • meter / second**2) := by sorry
Informal_statement: Question 59 A worker applies a constant horizontal force with magnitude 20 N to a box with mass 40 kg resting on a level, freshly waxed floor with negligible friction. What is the acceleration of the box? Answer: \[ \sum F_x = F + 0 + 0 = F = 20~\mathrm{N} = ma_x \] \[ \sum F_y = 0 + n - w = ma_y = 0 \] From the first equation, the $x$-component of acceleration is: \[ a_x = \frac{\sum F_x}{m} = \frac{20~\mathrm{N}}{40~\mathrm{kg}} = \frac{20~\mathrm{kg \cdot m/s^2}}{40~\mathrm{kg}} = 0.50~\mathrm{m/s^2} \]

Informal_statement: Question 66 Your firm needs to haul granite blocks up a 15° slope out of a quarry and to lower dirt into the quarry to fill the holes. You design a system in which a granite block on a cart with steel wheels (weight w1, including both block and cart) is pulled uphill on steel rails by a dirt-filled bucket (weight w2, including both dirt and bucket) that descends vertically into the quarry. How must the weights w1 and w2 be related in order for the system to move with constant speed? Ignore friction in the pulley and wheels, and ignore the weight of the cable. Answer: Applying $\sum F_y = 0$ to the bucket, we find: \[ \sum F_y = T + (-w_2) = 0 \quad \text{so} \quad T = w_2 \] Applying $\sum F_x = 0$ to the cart (and block) in Fig. 5.5d, we get: \[ \sum F_x = T + (-w_1 \sin 15^\circ) = 0 \quad \text{so} \quad T = w_1 \sin 15^\circ \] Equating the two expressions for $T$, we find: \[ w_2 = w_1 \sin 15^\circ = 0.26w_1 \]

  (hv_x : v_x = 6 • meter / second)
  (hv_0x : v_0x = 0 • meter / second)

  (hm : m = 200 • kilogram)

  (F_W = (300 : ℚ) • newton) := by sorry
Informal_statement: Question 67 An iceboat is at rest on a frictionless horizontal surface. Due to the blowing wind, $4.0~\mathrm{s}$ after the iceboat is released, it is moving to the right at $6.0~\mathrm{m/s}$ (about $22~\mathrm{km/h}$, or $13~\mathrm{mi/h}$). What constant horizontal force $F_W$ does the wind exert on the iceboat? The combined mass of the iceboat and rider is $200~\mathrm{kg}$. Answer: Respectively, we have: \[ \sum F_x = F_W = ma_x \] \[ \sum F_y = n + (-mg) = 0 \quad \text{so} \quad n = mg \] \[ v_x = v_{0x} + a_x t \] To find $F_W$, we first solve this third equation for $a_x$ and then substitute the result into the $\sum F_x$ equation: \[ a_x = \frac{v_x - v_{0x}}{t} = \frac{6.0~\mathrm{m/s} - 0}{4.0~\mathrm{s}} = 1.5~\mathrm{m/s^2} \] \[ F_W = ma_x = (200~\mathrm{kg})(1.5~\mathrm{m/s^2}) = 300~\mathrm{kg \cdot m/s^2} = 300~\mathrm{N} \]

Due to the blowing wind, $4.0~\mathrm{s}$ after the iceboat is released, it is moving to the right at $6.0~\mathrm{m/s}$ (about $22~\mathrm{km/h}$, or $13~\mathrm{mi/h}$). Suppose a constant horizontal friction force with magnitude $100~\mathrm{N}$ opposes the motion of the iceboat. In this case, what constant force $F_W$ must the wind exert on the iceboat to cause the same constant $x$-acceleration $a_x = 1.5~\mathrm{m/s^2}$? Answer: F_w = ma_x + f_friction = 200kg * 1.5m/s^2 + 100N = 400N

  (hy : y = 0 • meter)
  (hy_0 : y_0 = - 25 • meter)

  (ha_y : a_y = (v_y**2 - v_0y**2) / (2 • (y - y_0)))

Informal_statement: Question 69 An elevator and its load have a combined mass of $800~\mathrm{kg}$. The elevator is initially moving downward at $10.0~\mathrm{m/s}$; it slows to a stop with constant acceleration in a distance of $25.0~\mathrm{m}$. What is the tension $T$ in the supporting cable while the elevator is being brought to rest? Answer: First, let’s write out Newton’s second law. The tension force acts upward and the weight acts downward, so: \[ \sum F_y = T + (-w) = ma_y \] We solve for the target variable $T$: \[ T = w + ma_y = mg + ma_y = m(g + a_y) \] To determine $a_y$, we rewrite the constant-acceleration equation: \[ v_y^2 = v_{0y}^2 + 2a_y(y - y_0) \] \[ a_y = \frac{v_y^2 - v_{0y}^2}{2(y - y_0)} = \frac{(0)^2 - (-10.0~\mathrm{m/s})^2}{2(-25.0~\mathrm{m})} = +2.00~\mathrm{m/s^2} \] The acceleration is upward (positive), just as it should be. Now we can substitute the acceleration into the equation for the tension: \[ T = m(g + a_y) = (800~\mathrm{kg})(9.80~\mathrm{m/s^2} + 2.00~\mathrm{m/s^2}) = 9440~\mathrm{N} \]

The elevator is initially moving downward at $10.0~\mathrm{m/s}$; it slows to a stop with constant acceleration in a distance of $25.0~\mathrm{m}$. A 50.0 kg woman stands on a bathroom scale while riding in the elevator. While the elevator is moving downward with decreasing speed, what is the reading on the scale? Answer: \[ \sum F_y = n + (-mg) = ma_y \] \[ n = mg + ma_y = m(g + a_y) \] \[ n = (50.0~\mathrm{kg})(9.80~\mathrm{m/s^2} + 2.00~\mathrm{m/s^2}) = 590~\mathrm{N} \]

  (hT : T = (m_1 * m_2) / (m_1 + m_2) * g):

Informal_statement: Question 73 an air-track glider with mass m1 moves on a level, frictionless air track in the physics lab. The glider is connected to a lab weight with mass m2 by a light, flexible, nonstretching string that passes over a stationary, frictionless pulley. Find the acceleration of each object and the tension in the string. Answer: Newton’s second law gives: **Glider:** \[ \sum F_x = T = m_1 a_{1x} = m_1 a \] \[ \sum F_y = n + (-m_1 g) = m_1 a_{1y} = 0 \] **Lab weight:** \[ \sum F_y = m_2 g + (-T) = m_2 a_{2y} = m_2 a \] (There are no forces on the lab weight in the $x$-direction.) In these equations, we’ve used $a_{1y} = 0$ (the glider doesn’t accelerate vertically) and $a_{1x} = a_{2y} = a$. The $x$-equation for the glider and the equation for the lab weight give us two simultaneous equations for $T$ and $a$: **Glider:** \[ T = m_1 a \] **Lab weight:** \[ m_2 g - T = m_2 a \] We add the two equations to eliminate $T$, giving: \[ m_2 g = m_1 a + m_2 a = (m_1 + m_2)a \] and so the magnitude of each object’s acceleration is: \[ a = \frac{m_2}{m_1 + m_2} g \] Substituting this back into the glider equation $T= m_1 a$, we get \[ T = \frac{m_1 m_2}{m_1 + m_2} g \]

import Mathlib
import Aesop
import Mathlib.Tactic
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SI
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SIDeriv
import PHYSlib.Mechanics.Displacement
import PHYSlib.Mechanics.Motion
import PHYSlib.Thermodynamics.Volume
open BigOperators Real Nat Topology Rat UnitsSystem SI SI.Mechanics SI.Thermodynamics
unseal Rat.add Rat.mul Rat.sub Rat.inv
variable (m:Mass) (v:Speed) (F:Force) (p:Momentum) (E:Energy) (h:Length) (x:Displacement) (t:Time) (a:Acceleration)
theorem Mechanics_74_University
  (f_s_max f_k n w : Force)(μ_s μ_k : ℚ)
  (hw : w = 500 • newton)
  (hn : n = w)
  (hf_s_max : f_s_max = 230 • newton)
  (hf_k : f_k = 200 • newton)
  (hμ_s : μ_s = Scalar.val f_s_max / Scalar.val n)
  (hμ_k : μ_k = Scalar.val f_k / Scalar.val n):
  (μ_s = (0.46 : ℚ) ∧ μ_k = (0.40 : ℚ)) := by sorry
Informal_statement: Question 74 You want to move a 500 N crate across a level floor. To start the crate moving, you have to pull with a 230 N horizontal force. Once the crate starts to move, you can keep it moving at constant velocity with only 200 N. What are the coefficients of static and kinetic friction? Answer: Just before the crate starts to move, we have from Eqs. (5.1): \[ \sum F_x = T + \big(- (f_s)_{\text{max}}\big) = 0 \quad \text{so} \quad (f_s)_{\text{max}} = T = 230~\mathrm{N} \] \[ \sum F_y = n + (-w) = 0 \quad \text{so} \quad n = w = 500~\mathrm{N} \] Now we solve Eq. (5.4), \((f_s)_{\text{max}} = \mu_s n\), for the value of \(\mu_s\): \[ \mu_s = \frac{(f_s)_{\text{max}}}{n} = \frac{230~\mathrm{N}}{500~\mathrm{N}} = 0.46 \] After the crate starts to move (Fig. 5.20c), we have: \[ \sum F_x = T + \big(- f_k\big) = 0 \quad \text{so} \quad f_k = T = 200~\mathrm{N} \] \[ \sum F_y = n + (-w) = 0 \quad \text{so} \quad n = w = 500~\mathrm{N} \] Using \(f_k = \mu_k n\) from Eq. (5.3), we find: \[ \mu_k = \frac{f_k}{n} = \frac{200~\mathrm{N}}{500~\mathrm{N}} = 0.40 \]

  (f_s : Force)(T : Force)(w : Force)(n : Force)(μ_s : ℚ)
  (hw : w = 500 • newton)

  (f_s = (50 : ℚ) • newton) := by sorry
Informal_statement: Question 75 You want to move a 500 N crate across a level floor. To start the crate moving, you have to pull with a 230 N horizontal force. Once the crate starts to move, you can keep it moving at constant velocity with only 200 N. What is the friction force if the crate is at rest on the surface and a horizontal force of 50 N is applied to it? Answer: \[ \sum F_x = T + \big(-f_s\big) = 0 \quad \text{so} \quad f_s = T = 50~\mathrm{N} \]

Header:

Theorem:
theorem Mechanics_76_University
  (T n w : Force)(μ_k : ℚ)(θ : PhysAngle)
  (hw : w = 500 • newton)
  (hμ_k : μ_k = (0.40:ℚ))
  (hθ : θ = (30 * π / 180) • radian)
  (hT : T = μ_k • w / (cos (Scalar.val θ) + μ_k • sin (Scalar.val θ)))
  (hn : n = w - sin (Scalar.val θ) • T):
  (T = (188 : ℚ) • newton ∧ n = (406 : ℚ) • newton) := by sorry

import Mathlib

unseal Rat.add Rat.mul Rat.sub Rat.inv
variable (m:Mass) (v:Speed) (F:Force) (p:Momentum) (E:Energy) (h:Length) (x:Displacement) (t:Time) (a:Acceleration)
theorem Mechanics_76_University
  (T n w : Force)(μ_k : ℚ)(θ : PhysAngle)
  (hw : w = 500 • newton)
  (hμ_k : μ_k = (0.40:ℚ))
  (hθ : θ = (30 * π / 180) • radian)
  (hT : T = μ_k • w / (cos (Scalar.val θ) + μ_k • sin (Scalar.val θ)))
  (hn : n = w - sin (Scalar.val θ) • T):
  (T = (188 : ℚ) • newton ∧ n = (406 : ℚ) • newton) := by sorry
Informal_statement: Question 76 You want to move a 500 N crate across a level floor. To start the crate moving, you have to pull with a 230 N horizontal force. Once the crate starts to move, you can keep it moving at constant velocity with only 200 N. Suppose you move the crate by pulling upward on the rope at an angle of 30° above the horizontal. How hard must you pull to keep it moving with constant velocity? Assume that m_k = 0.40. Answer: \[ T = \frac{\mu_k w}{\cos 30^\circ + \mu_k \sin 30^\circ} = 188~\mathrm{N} \] We can substitute this result into either of the original equations to obtain $n$. If we use the second equation, we get: \[ n = w - T \sin 30^\circ = (500~\mathrm{N}) - (188~\mathrm{N}) \sin 30^\circ = 406~\mathrm{N} \]

  (w n : Force)(α : PhysAngle)(μ_k : ℚ)

import Mathlib
import Aesop
import Mathlib.Tactic

import PHYSlib.Foundations.SIDeriv

import PHYSlib.Mechanics.Motion
import PHYSlib.Thermodynamics.Volume
open BigOperators Real Nat Topology Rat UnitsSystem SI SI.Mechanics SI.Thermodynamics
unseal Rat.add Rat.mul Rat.sub Rat.inv
variable (m:Mass) (v:Speed) (F:Force) (p:Momentum) (E:Energy) (h:Length) (x:Displacement) (t:Time) (a:Acceleration)
theorem Mechanics_77_University
  (w n : Force)(α : PhysAngle)(μ_k : ℚ)
  (hn : 0 = sin (Scalar.val α) • w - μ_k • n)
  (hn2 : 0 = n - cos (Scalar.val α) • w):
  (μ_k = tan (Scalar.val α)) := by sorry
Informal_statement: Question 77 A toboggan loaded with students (total weight $w$) slides down a snow-covered hill that slopes at a constant angle $\alpha$. There is now a nonzero coefficient of kinetic friction mk. The slope has just the right angle to make the toboggan slide with constant velocity. Find this angle in terms of w and mk. Answer: The equilibrium conditions are: \[ \sum F_x = w \sin \alpha + \big(-f_k\big) = w \sin \alpha - \mu_k n = 0 \] \[ \sum F_y = n + \big(-w \cos \alpha\big) = 0 \] Rearranging these two equations, we get: \[ \mu_k n = w \sin \alpha \quad \text{and} \quad n = w \cos \alpha \] The normal force is \textit{not} equal to the weight. We eliminate $n$ by dividing the first of these equations by the second, with the result: \[ \mu_k = \frac{\sin \alpha}{\cos \alpha} = \tan \alpha \quad \text{so} \quad \alpha = \arctan \mu_k \]

    (q : Charge) (m : Mass) (E :  Scalar (force_unit - charge_unit)) (d : Length)

Statement:
import Mathlib
import Aesop
import Mathlib.Tactic
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SI
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SIDeriv
import PHYSlib.Electromagnetism.Couloubconstant
import PHYSlib.Mechanics.Displacement
import PHYSlib.Mechanics.Motion
import Mathlib.Algebra.Group.MinimalAxioms
open BigOperators Real Nat Topology Rat UnitsSystem SI SI.Electromagnetism
unseal Rat.add Rat.mul Rat.sub Rat.inv
variable (m:Mass) (v:Speed) (F:Force) (p:Momentum) (E:Energy) (h:Length) (x:Displacement) (t:Time) (a:Acceleration)
theorem Electromagnetism_8_University
    (q : Charge) (m : Mass) (E :  Scalar (force_unit - charge_unit)) (d : Length)
    (hq : q = (-1.60e-19:ℝ) • coulomb)
    (hm : m = (9.11e-31:ℝ) • kilogram)
    (hE : E = (1e4:ℝ) • StandardUnit _)
    (hd : d = (0.01:ℝ) • meter)
    (v : Speed) (Kin : Energy)(hv':v≥ 0)
    (hv : v**2 = -2 • E*q*d/m)
    (hK : Kin = ((1/2):ℝ) • m * v**2)
    :
    v = ((√(32000000000000000 / 911)):ℝ) • (meter / second) ∧
    Kin = (1 / 62500000000000000 :ℝ) • joule := by sorry
Proof:
have hval1:(v**2).val=(v.val)^2:=by
        exact rfl
    split_ands
    apply UnitsSystem.Scalar.ext_iff.mp at hv
    rw[hval1] at hv
    have hsqrt:v.val=√((-2 • E * q * d / m).val):=by
        refine Eq.symm ((fun {x y} => sqrt_eq_cases.mpr) ?_)
        left
        split_ands
        rw[← pow_two,hv]
        exact hv'
    refine Scalar.ext_iff.mpr ?_
    rw[hsqrt,hE,hq,hd,hm]
    simp
    ring_nf
    simp
    ring_nf
    refine Scalar.ext_iff.mpr ?_
    rw[hK]
    simp
    rw[← hval1,hv,hE,hq,hd,hm,UnitsSystem.Scalar.val_hDiv,UnitsSystem.Scalar.val_hMul
        ,UnitsSystem.Scalar.val_hMul]
    simp
    ring_nf
Informal_statement: Question 8 When the terminals of a battery are connected to two parallel conducting plates with a small gap between them, the resulting charges on the plates produce a nearly uniform electric field ES between the plates. If the plates are 1.0 cm apart and are connected to a 100 volt battery, the field is vertically upward and has magnitude E= 1.00 x 104 N/C. (b) Whatspeed and kinetic energy does it acquire while traveling 1.0 cm to thelower plate?

import PHYSlib.Mechanics.Displacement
import Mathlib.Algebra.Group.MinimalAxioms
import Mathlib.Analysis.Calculus.Deriv.Basic
open BigOperators Real Nat Topology Rat UnitsSystem SI SI.Mechanics
unseal Rat.add Rat.mul Rat.sub Rat.inv
variable (m:Mass) (v:Speed) (F:Force) (p:Momentum) (E:Energy) (h:Length) (x:Displacement) (t:Time) (a:Acceleration)
theorem Waves_1_University
  (h :Length)(v : Speed)(f : Frequency)
  (h_v: v = 344 • meter / second)
  (h_f: f = 262 • hertz )
  (hh: h = v / f):
  h = (344/262 : ℚ) • meter := by sorry
Proof: simp [←Scalar.val_inj, h_v, h_f, hh, meter, second, hertz]
Informal_statement: Question 1 Sound waves are longitudinal waves in air. The speed of sound depends on temperature; at 20°C it is 344 m/s. What is the wavelength of a sound wave in air at 20°C if the frequency is 262 Hz (the approximate frequency of middle C on a piano)?

  (t1 t2 t3 : Time)(ht1':t1.val>0)(ht2':t2.val>0)(ht3':t3.val>0)(h':AC.val>0)
  (α : ℝ)(hα : α ∈ Set.Ioo 0 (π/2))
  (h_AB : AB = Real.sin α • AC)
  (h_BC : BC = Real.cos α • AC)
  (ht1 : t1 ** 2 = (2 • AB / g))
  (ht2 : t2 ** 2 = ((Real.cot α/2) • BC / g))
  (ht3 : t3 ** 2 = (2 • AC / (Real.sin α • g)))
  (h_sum : t1 + t2 = t3) :
  Real.sin α + (1 / 2 : ℝ) * Real.cos α = 1 := by sorry
Informal_statement: EXAMPLE Ch1_Q1 Q: For right triangle ABC with hypotenuse AC and angle α between AC and horizontal BC, a point mass moves from A to C starting from rest. When moving along path AB then BC, times are t₁ and t₂; when moving along AC, time is t₃. Given t₁ + t₂ = t₃, show that \sin \alpha + \frac{1}{2} \cos \alpha = 1.

theorem Ch1_Q2
  {v : Time → Speed}
  (hdif:Differentiable ℝ v)
  (k : Scalar (-time_unit))
  (v0 : Speed)
  (n : ℝ)
  (hk_pos : 0 < k.val)
  (hn : n > 1)
  (hv_diff : physderiv v=physmapcast (physmapsmul v (-k)) _ _ (by module) (by module))
  (hv0 : v (0 • second) = v0)
  (h_total : Tendsto
    (fun T : ℝ => ∫ t in (0:ℝ)..T, (v ⟨t⟩).val)
    atTop (𝓝 x_total))
  (t₁ : Time) (ht₁ : v t₁ = v0 / n)
  : ∫ t in (0:ℝ)..t₁.val, ((mathcast v) t) / x_total = 1 - 1 / n := by sorry

Let the particle's speed reduce to v₀ / n (n > 1). Show that the ratio of the distance traveled to the total distance is 1 - 1/n.

  (hα₁_range : 0 < α₁ ∧ α₁ < Real.pi / 2)
  (hα₂_range : 0 < α₂ ∧ α₂ < Real.pi / 2)
  (t₁ t₂ : Time)

  (ht₂ : t₂ = ((2:ℝ) • vy₂) / g)
  (hR₁ : R = vx₁ * t₁)
  (hR₂ : R = vx₂ * t₂)
  :
  t₁ * t₂ = ((2:ℝ) • R) / g := by sorry

Name: competition_mechanics_Ch1_Q6
Header:
import Mathlib
import Aesop
import Mathlib.Tactic
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SI
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SIDeriv
open BigOperators Real Nat Topology Filter Rat UnitsSystem SI
unseal Rat.add Rat.mul Rat.sub Rat.inv
Theorem:
theorem Ch1_Q6
  (v_water : Speed)
  (angle : ℝ)
  (dist_wall wall_height : Length)
  (h_pos : 0 < dist_wall.val ∧ 0 < wall_height.val)
  (traj : Length → Length)
  (traj_eq : ∀ x, traj x = (Real.tan angle) • x - (1 / (Real.cos angle ^ 2)) • g * x ** 2 / (2 • v_water ** 2))
  (x_wall : Length
:=dist_wall)
  (y_wall : Length
:=wall_height)
  (hits_wall : traj x_wall = y_wall)
  (minimal_v : ∀ v angle',
    (∀ x, (Real.tan angle') • x - (1 / (Real.cos angle' ^ 2)) • g * x ** 2 / (2 • v ** 2) = traj x) →
    traj x_wall = y_wall →
    v_water ≤ v)
  :
  v_water ** 2 = g * (dist_wall + wall_height)
  ∧ angle = Real.pi / 4 + (1 / 2) * Real.arctan (wall_height.val / dist_wall.val) := by sorry
Statement:

  (h_pos : 0 < dist_wall.val ∧ 0 < wall_height.val)
  (traj : Length → Length)
  (traj_eq : ∀ x, traj x = (Real.tan angle) • x - (1 / (Real.cos angle ^ 2)) • g * x ** 2 / (2 • v_water ** 2))
  (x_wall : Length
:=dist_wall)
  (y_wall : Length
:=wall_height)
  (hits_wall : traj x_wall = y_wall)
  (minimal_v : ∀ v angle',
    (∀ x, (Real.tan angle') • x - (1 / (Real.cos angle' ^ 2)) • g * x ** 2 / (2 • v ** 2) = traj x) →
    traj x_wall = y_wall →
    v_water ≤ v)

  ∧ angle = Real.pi / 4 + (1 / 2) * Real.arctan (wall_height.val / dist_wall.val) := by sorry
Informal_statement: EXAMPLE Ch1_Q6 Q: A water gun is at a perpendicular distance d from a vertical wall of height h. The gun fires a stream of water with initial speed v_0 at an angle α above the horizontal. Prove that the minimum initial speed v_0 required to just clear the top of the wall satisfies v_0^2 = g (d + h), and the corresponding launch angle is α = 45^\circ + \frac{1}{2} \arctan \frac{h}{d}.

Name: competition_mechanics_Ch1_Q7
Header:
import Mathlib
import Aesop
import Mathlib.Tactic
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SI
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SIDeriv
open BigOperators Real Nat Topology Filter Rat UnitsSystem SI
unseal Rat.add Rat.mul Rat.sub Rat.inv
Theorem:
theorem Ch1_Q7
  (H : Length)
  (v_ball1 v_ball2 : Speed)
  (h_pos : 0 < H.val ∧ 0 < v_ball2.val ∧ 0 < v_ball1.val)
  (R : Length)
  (x_pole : Length)
  (h_pole : Length)
  (traj_ball1 : Length → Length)
  (traj_ball2 : Length → Length)
  (ball1_eq : ∀ x, traj_ball1 x = H - (g / (2 • v_ball1 ** 2)) * x ** 2)
  (ball2_eq : ∀ x, traj_ball2 x = H - (g / (2 • v_ball2 ** 2)) * (x - (2/3) • R) ** 2)
  (ball1_land : traj_ball1 R = 0)
  (ball2_first_land : traj_ball2 (R/3) = 0)
  (pole_ball1 : traj_ball1 x_pole = h_pole)
  (pole_ball2 : traj_ball2 x_pole = h_pole)
  (ratio_unique : ∀ v1' v2',
    (∀ x, H - (g / (2 • v1' ** 2)) * x ** 2 = traj_ball1 x) →
    (∀ x, H - (g / (2 • v2' ** 2)) * (x - (2/3) • R) ** 2 = traj_ball2 x) →
    v_ball1 / v_ball2 = v1' / v2')
  :v_ball1.val / v_ball2.val = 3 ∧ x_pole = R / 2 ∧ h_pole = (3/4) • H := by sorry
Statement:
import Mathlib
import Aesop
import Mathlib.Tactic
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SI
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SIDeriv
open BigOperators Real Nat Topology Filter Rat UnitsSystem SI
unseal Rat.add Rat.mul Rat.sub Rat.inv
theorem Ch1_Q7
  (H : Length)
  (v_ball1 v_ball2 : Speed)
  (h_pos : 0 < H.val ∧ 0 < v_ball2.val ∧ 0 < v_ball1.val)
  (R : Length)
  (x_pole : Length)
  (h_pole : Length)
  (traj_ball1 : Length → Length)
  (traj_ball2 : Length → Length)
  (ball1_eq : ∀ x, traj_ball1 x = H - (g / (2 • v_ball1 ** 2)) * x ** 2)
  (ball2_eq : ∀ x, traj_ball2 x = H - (g / (2 • v_ball2 ** 2)) * (x - (2/3) • R) ** 2)
  (ball1_land : traj_ball1 R = 0)
  (ball2_first_land : traj_ball2 (R/3) = 0)
  (pole_ball1 : traj_ball1 x_pole = h_pole)
  (pole_ball2 : traj_ball2 x_pole = h_pole)
  (ratio_unique : ∀ v1' v2',
    (∀ x, H - (g / (2 • v1' ** 2)) * x ** 2 = traj_ball1 x) →
    (∀ x, H - (g / (2 • v2' ** 2)) * (x - (2/3) • R) ** 2 = traj_ball2 x) →
    v_ball1 / v_ball2 = v1' / v2')
  :v_ball1.val / v_ball2.val = 3 ∧ x_pole = R / 2 ∧ h_pole = (3/4) • H := by sorry
Informal_statement: EXAMPLE Ch1_Q7 Q: Two balls are thrown horizontally from height H with horizontal speeds v₁ > v₂. Ball 1 passes the top of a vertical pole at x_P and lands at distance R. Ball 2 first hits the ground elastically, then passes the same pole and lands at R. Prove the values of v₁ / v₂, x_P, and the pole height h.

Name: competition_mechanics_Ch1_Q8
Header:
import Mathlib
import Aesop
import Mathlib.Tactic
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SI
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SIDeriv
open BigOperators Real Nat Topology Filter Rat UnitsSystem SI
unseal Rat.add Rat.mul Rat.sub Rat.inv
Theorem:
theorem Ch1_Q8
  (v_missile v_air : Speed)
  (L : Length)
  (h_pos : 0 < L.val ∧ v_missile.val > v_air.val)
  (t : Time)
  (x_missile y_missile : Time → Length)
  (hdif1:Differentiable ℝ x_missile)
  (y_aircraft : Time → Length
:=fun t => v_air * t)
  (slope_condition : ∀ t : Time,(y_aircraft t - y_missile t)
    = ((physderiv y_missile) t)/((physderiv x_missile) t)*(L - x_missile t))
  (speed_condition :∀t₁,
      (physderiv (fun t=>(x_missile t)** 2)) t₁
    + (physderiv (fun t=>(y_missile t)** 2)) t₁
      = (v_missile**2).cast (by_module))
  (dx_dt_nonzero : ∀ t : Time,
      (physderiv x_missile) t ≠ 0)
  (γ : ℝ
:=v_air.val / v_missile.val)
  (t_hit : Time
:=L / ((1 - γ ^ 2) • v_missile))
  :
  y_missile t_hit = y_aircraft t_hit := by sorry
Statement:
import Mathlib
import Aesop
import Mathlib.Tactic
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SI
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SIDeriv
open BigOperators Real Nat Topology Filter Rat UnitsSystem SI
unseal Rat.add Rat.mul Rat.sub Rat.inv
theorem Ch1_Q8
  (v_missile v_air : Speed)
  (L : Length)
  (h_pos : 0 < L.val ∧ v_missile.val > v_air.val)
  (t : Time)
  (x_missile y_missile : Time → Length)
  (hdif1:Differentiable ℝ x_missile)
  (y_aircraft : Time → Length
:=fun t => v_air * t)
  (slope_condition : ∀ t : Time,(y_aircraft t - y_missile t)
    = ((physderiv y_missile) t)/((physderiv x_missile) t)*(L - x_missile t))
  (speed_condition :∀t₁,
      (physderiv (fun t=>(x_missile t)** 2)) t₁
    + (physderiv (fun t=>(y_missile t)** 2)) t₁
      = (v_missile**2).cast (by_module))
  (dx_dt_nonzero : ∀ t : Time,
      (physderiv x_missile) t ≠ 0)
  (γ : ℝ
:=v_air.val / v_missile.val)
  (t_hit : Time
:=L / ((1 - γ ^ 2) • v_missile))
  :
  y_missile t_hit = y_aircraft t_hit := by sorry
Informal_statement: EXAMPLE Ch1_Q8 Q: An aircraft takes off vertically from the ground with a constant speed \(v_f\). At a horizontal distance \(L\) from the aircraft's launch point, a missile is launched simultaneously with a constant speed \(v > v_f\), and at every instant it is directed toward the aircraft's position. Prove that the missile's trajectory satisfies the differential equation \[ (L-x) \frac{d^2 y}{dx^2} = \frac{v_f}{v} \sqrt{1 + \left( \frac{dy}{dx} \right)^2 } \] and determine the time \(t^*\) when the missile hits the aircraft: \[ t^* = \frac{L}{(1 - \gamma^2) v}, \quad \gamma := \frac{v_f}{v}. \]

Name: competition_mechanics_Ch1_Q9
Header:
import Mathlib
import Aesop
import Mathlib.Tactic
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SI
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SIDeriv
open BigOperators Real Nat Topology Filter Rat UnitsSystem SI
unseal Rat.add Rat.mul Rat.sub Rat.inv
Theorem:
theorem Ch1_Q9
  (v : Speed)
  (r : Length)
  (h_pos : 0 < v.val ∧ 0 < r.val)
  (t : Time)
  (l : Time → Length)
  (phi : Time → ℝ)
  (x_ball y_ball : Time → Length)
  (l_def : ∀ t, (l t)**2 = (2 • r * v * t))
  (phi_def : ∀ t, phi t = (l t / r).val)
  :
  (∃ a_ball : Time → Acceleration,
    (∀ t, (a_ball t)**2 = v**2 * ((v / (2 • r * t)))) ∧
    (∀ t,
      x_ball t = (Real.cos (phi t) + phi t * Real.sin (phi t)) • r ∧
      y_ball t = (Real.sin (phi t) - phi t * Real.cos (phi t)) • r)
  ) := by sorry
Statement:
import Mathlib
import Aesop
import Mathlib.Tactic
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SI
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SIDeriv
open BigOperators Real Nat Topology Filter Rat UnitsSystem SI
unseal Rat.add Rat.mul Rat.sub Rat.inv
theorem Ch1_Q9
  (v : Speed)
  (r : Length)
  (h_pos : 0 < v.val ∧ 0 < r.val)
  (t : Time)
  (l : Time → Length)
  (phi : Time → ℝ)
  (x_ball y_ball : Time → Length)
  (l_def : ∀ t, (l t)**2 = (2 • r * v * t))
  (phi_def : ∀ t, phi t = (l t / r).val)
  :
  (∃ a_ball : Time → Acceleration,
    (∀ t, (a_ball t)**2 = v**2 * ((v / (2 • r * t)))) ∧
    (∀ t,
      x_ball t = (Real.cos (phi t) + phi t * Real.sin (phi t)) • r ∧
      y_ball t = (Real.sin (phi t) - phi t * Real.cos (phi t)) • r)
  ) := by sorry
Informal_statement: EXAMPLE Ch1_Q9 Q: A small ball is attached to the end of a lightweight, non-stretchable string fully wound around a stationary vertical cylinder of radius r. The ball is struck so that it moves horizontally with initial speed v, perpendicular to the cylinder. As the string unwinds without slipping, the ball stays in the horizontal plane. Prove that the acceleration of the ball at time t is a(t) = \frac{v \sqrt{v}}{\sqrt{2 r t}}, directed toward the tangent point of the cylinder, and that its trajectory satisfies the parametric equations x(t) = r (cos φ + φ sin φ), y(t) = r (sin φ - φ cos φ), with φ = \sqrt{2 v t / r}.

Name: competition_mechanics_Ch1_Q10
Header:
import Mathlib
import Aesop
import Mathlib.Tactic
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SI
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SIDeriv
open BigOperators Real Nat Topology Filter Rat UnitsSystem SI
unseal Rat.add Rat.mul Rat.sub Rat.inv
Theorem:
theorem Ch1_Q10
  (v : Speed)
  (ω : Scalar (-time_unit))
  (t : Time)
  (r_ring : Time → Length
:=fun t => v * t)
  (φ_ring : Time → Dimensionless
:=fun t => ω * t)
  (x_ring : Time → Length
:=fun t => Real.cos ((φ_ring t).val) • r_ring t)
  (y_ring : Time → Length
:=fun t => Real.sin ((φ_ring t).val) • r_ring t )
  (v_r : Time → Speed
:=physderiv r_ring)
  (v_φ : Time → Speed
:=fun t => r_ring t * ω)
  (a_r : Time → Acceleration
:=fun t => ((physderiv v_r) t)  - ((r_ring t) * ω**2).cast (by module))
  (a_φ : Time → Acceleration
:=fun t => 2 • v * ω)
  :
  (∀ t, r_ring t = (v / ω) * φ_ring t) ∧
  (∀ t, v_r t = v ∧ v_φ t = v * ω * t) ∧
  (∀ t, a_r t = -v * ω**2 * t ∧ a_φ t = 2 • v * ω) := by sorry
Statement:
import Mathlib
import Aesop
import Mathlib.Tactic
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SI
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SIDeriv
open BigOperators Real Nat Topology Filter Rat UnitsSystem SI
unseal Rat.add Rat.mul Rat.sub Rat.inv
theorem Ch1_Q10
  (v : Speed)
  (ω : Scalar (-time_unit))
  (t : Time)
  (r_ring : Time → Length
:=fun t => v * t)
  (φ_ring : Time → Dimensionless
:=fun t => ω * t)
  (x_ring : Time → Length
:=fun t => Real.cos ((φ_ring t).val) • r_ring t)
  (y_ring : Time → Length
:=fun t => Real.sin ((φ_ring t).val) • r_ring t )
  (v_r : Time → Speed
:=physderiv r_ring)
  (v_φ : Time → Speed
:=fun t => r_ring t * ω)
  (a_r : Time → Acceleration
:=fun t => ((physderiv v_r) t)  - ((r_ring t) * ω**2).cast (by module))
  (a_φ : Time → Acceleration
:=fun t => 2 • v * ω)
  :
  (∀ t, r_ring t = (v / ω) * φ_ring t) ∧
  (∀ t, v_r t = v ∧ v_φ t = v * ω * t) ∧
  (∀ t, a_r t = -v * ω**2 * t ∧ a_φ t = 2 • v * ω) := by sorry
Informal_statement: EXAMPLE Ch1_Q10 Q: A slender rod rotates uniformly with angular velocity ω around a fixed endpoint O in a plane. A small ring moves along the rod with constant speed v relative to the rod. At time t = 0, the ring is located at the endpoint O. Prove that the trajectory of the ring is an Archimedean spiral: r = (v / ω) φ where r is the distance from O and φ is the angle the rod makes with a fixed axis.

Name: competition_mechanics_Ch1_Q11
Header:
import Mathlib
import Aesop
import Mathlib.Tactic
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SI
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SIDeriv
open BigOperators Real Nat Topology Filter Rat UnitsSystem SI
unseal Rat.add Rat.mul Rat.sub Rat.inv
Theorem:
theorem Ch1_Q11
  (v1 v2 : Speed)
  (l : Length)
  (α : ℝ)
  (h_pos : 0 < l.val ∧ 0 < v1.val ∧ 0 < v2.val ∧ 0 < α ∧ α < Real.pi)
  (t : Time)
  (xA yA xB yB : Time → Length)
  (posA_x : ∀ t, xA t = v1 * t)
  (posA_y : ∀ t, yA t = 0)
  (posB_x : ∀ t, xB t = l -  Real.cos α • v2 * t/1)
  (posB_y : ∀ t, yB t = Real.sin α • v2 * t)
  (dist : Time → Length)
  (h_dist : ∀ t:Time, dist t**2 = (xB t - xA t)**2 + (yB t - yA t)**2)
  (dist_min_sq : Scalar (2 • length_unit)
:=l**2 * ((Real.sin α) ^ 2 • v2**2) / (v1**2 + (Real.cos α) • (v1 * v2)/1 + v2**2))
  :
  ∃ t_min : Time, (dist t_min)**2 = dist_min_sq ∧ ∀ t':Time, dist t' ≥ dist t_min := by sorry

  (t : Time)

  (posA_y : ∀ t, yA t = 0)
  (posB_x : ∀ t, xB t = l -  Real.cos α • v2 * t/1)
  (posB_y : ∀ t, yB t = Real.sin α • v2 * t)
  (dist : Time → Length)
  (h_dist : ∀ t:Time, dist t**2 = (xB t - xA t)**2 + (yB t - yA t)**2)
  (dist_min_sq : Scalar (2 • length_unit)
:=l**2 * ((Real.sin α) ^ 2 • v2**2) / (v1**2 + (Real.cos α) • (v1 * v2)/1 + v2**2))
  :
  ∃ t_min : Time, (dist t_min)**2 = dist_min_sq ∧ ∀ t':Time, dist t' ≥ dist t_min := by sorry
Informal_statement: EXAMPLE Ch1_Q11 Q: Particles $A$ and $B$ start simultaneously from points $A$ and $B$, respectively. Particle $A$ moves with constant velocity $\boldsymbol{v}_1$ along line $AB$, and particle $B$ moves with constant velocity $v_2$ along line $BC$, where the angle between $BC$ and $AB$ is $\alpha$. The initial separation between $A$ and $B$ along $AB$ is $l$. Prove that the minimum distance between the two particles is \[ r_{\min} = \frac{v_2 \sin \alpha}{\sqrt{v_1^2 + v_2^2 + 2 v_1 v_2 \cos \alpha}} \, l. \]

Name: competition_mechanics_Ch1_Q12
Header:
import Mathlib
import Aesop
import Mathlib.Tactic
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SI
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SIDeriv
open BigOperators Real Nat Topology Filter Rat UnitsSystem SI
unseal Rat.add Rat.mul Rat.sub Rat.inv
Theorem:
theorem Ch1_Q12
  (v0 vr : Speed)
  (L : Length)
  (h_pos : 0 < v0.val ∧ 0 < vr.val ∧ 0 < L.val)
  (t : Time)
  (y_out : Time → Length)
  (x_out : Time → Length)
  (y_ret : Time → Length)
  (x_ret : Time → Length)
  (y_out_def : ∀ t, y_out t = vr * t)
  (x_out_def : ∀ t, x_out t = (v0 / (L * vr)) * (y_out t) ** 2)
  (y_turn : Length
:=L / 4)
  (x_turn : Length
:=x_out (y_turn / vr))
  (y_ret_def : ∀ t, y_ret t = y_turn - (vr / 2) * t)
  (x_ret_def : ∀ t, x_ret t = (-2) • v0 / (L * vr) * (y_ret t) ** 2 + 3 • v0 * L / (16 • vr))
  (x2 : Length
:=x_ret (2 • y_turn / vr))
  :
  x_out (y_turn / vr) = x_turn ∧
  x_ret 0 = 3 •  v0 * L / (16 •  vr) ∧
  x2 = 3 •  v0 * L / (16 •  vr) := by sorry

  (t : Time)
  (y_out : Time → Length)
  (x_out : Time → Length)
  (y_ret : Time → Length)
  (x_ret : Time → Length)
  (y_out_def : ∀ t, y_out t = vr * t)
  (x_out_def : ∀ t, x_out t = (v0 / (L * vr)) * (y_out t) ** 2)
  (y_turn : Length
:=L / 4)
  (x_turn : Length
:=x_out (y_turn / vr))
  (y_ret_def : ∀ t, y_ret t = y_turn - (vr / 2) * t)
  (x_ret_def : ∀ t, x_ret t = (-2) • v0 / (L * vr) * (y_ret t) ** 2 + 3 • v0 * L / (16 • vr))
  (x2 : Length
:=x_ret (2 • y_turn / vr))
  :

Informal_statement: EXAMPLE Ch1_Q12 Q: A river has width \(L\) and a flow velocity proportional to the distance from the shore, zero at the banks and \(v_0\) at the center. A small boat crosses the river with constant speed \(v_r\) perpendicular to the flow. When the boat reaches a distance \(L/4\) from the starting shore, it turns back with speed \(v_r/2\) perpendicular to the flow. Prove that the trajectory of the boat satisfies x = \frac{v_0}{L v_r} y^2 (outgoing) and x = -\frac{2 v_0}{L (v_r/2)} y^2 + \frac{3 v_0 L}{16 v_r} (returning), and that the distance from the original starting point when the boat returns is x_2 = \frac{3 v_0 L}{16 v_r}.

Name: competition_mechanics_Ch1_Q13
Header:
import Mathlib

import Mathlib.Tactic

import PHYSlib.Foundations.SIDeriv
open BigOperators Real Nat Topology Filter Rat UnitsSystem SI
unseal Rat.add Rat.mul Rat.sub Rat.inv
Theorem:
theorem Ch1_Q13
  (v0 v' : Speed)
  (r0 : Length)
  (φ0 : ℝ)
  (h_pos : 0 < v0.val ∧ 0 < v'.val ∧ 0 < r0.val ∧ 0 < φ0 ∧ φ0 < Real.pi)
  (t : Time)
  (r : Time → Length)
  (phi : Time → ℝ)
  (hdif1:Differentiable ℝ r)
  (hdif2:Differentiable ℝ phi)
  (vel_r : Time → Speed
:=fun t => -v' + Real.cos (phi t) • v0 )
  (vel_φ : Time → Speed
:=fun t => -Real.sin (phi t) • v0)
  (r_traj : physderiv r=vel_r)
  (φ_traj : ∀ t, deriv (λ t' => (phi (t' • second))) t.val = (vel_φ t / (r t)).val)
  :
  ∀ t, (r t / r0).val = (Real.tan ((phi t) / 2) / Real.tan (φ0 / 2)) ^ ((v'/ v0).val)  * (Real.sin φ0 / Real.sin (phi t)) := by sorry
Statement:

import Mathlib.Tactic

  (v0 v' : Speed)
  (r0 : Length)
  (φ0 : ℝ)
  (h_pos : 0 < v0.val ∧ 0 < v'.val ∧ 0 < r0.val ∧ 0 < φ0 ∧ φ0 < Real.pi)
  (t : Time)
  (r : Time → Length)
  (phi : Time → ℝ)
  (hdif1:Differentiable ℝ r)
  (hdif2:Differentiable ℝ phi)
  (vel_r : Time → Speed
:=fun t => -v' + Real.cos (phi t) • v0 )
  (vel_φ : Time → Speed
:=fun t => -Real.sin (phi t) • v0)
  (r_traj : physderiv r=vel_r)
  (φ_traj : ∀ t, deriv (λ t' => (phi (t' • second))) t.val = (vel_φ t / (r t)).val)
  :
  ∀ t, (r t / r0).val = (Real.tan ((phi t) / 2) / Real.tan (φ0 / 2)) ^ ((v'/ v0).val)  * (Real.sin φ0 / Real.sin (phi t)) := by sorry
Informal_statement: EXAMPLE Ch1_Q13 Q: A straight river has width d, with water flowing at constant speed v_0. A boat starts at point A on the riverbank and always moves towards point O on the opposite bank with a speed v' relative to the water (v' > v_0). Denote AO = r_0 and angle ∠AOP = φ_0. Prove that the trajectory of the boat in polar coordinates (r, φ) with origin at O and polar axis along OP satisfies \[ \frac{r}{r_0} = \left( \frac{\tan \frac{\varphi}{2}}{\tan \frac{\varphi_0}{2}} \right)^{v'/v_0} \frac{\sin \varphi_0}{\sin \varphi}. \] In particular, if O is directly opposite A (r_0 = d, φ_0 = π/2), show that \[ r = \frac{d}{\sin \varphi} \left( \tan \frac{\varphi}{2} \right)^{v'/v_0}. \]

Let point A on the rim of the wheel form an angle θ with the vertical line through the wheel's center. Prove that the magnitude of the absolute velocity of point A is v_A = 2 v_c \sin(\theta/2)

Name: competition_mechanics_Ch2_Q1
Header:
import Mathlib
import Aesop
import Mathlib.Tactic
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SI
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SIDeriv
open BigOperators Real Nat Topology Filter Rat UnitsSystem SI
unseal Rat.add Rat.mul Rat.sub Rat.inv
Theorem:
theorem Ch2_Q1
  (M m : Mass)
  (μ : ℝ)
  (n : ℝ)
  (θ_total : ℝ
:=2 * Real.pi * n)
  (T : ℝ → Force)
  (hdif:Differentiable ℝ T)
  (h_pos : 0 < M.val ∧ 0 < m.val ∧ 0 < μ)
  (hM_gt_m : M.val > m.val)
  (T_light_def : T 0 = m * g)
  (T_heavy_def : T θ_total = M * g)
  (capstan_differential : ∀ θ : ℝ, deriv (fun θ' => (T θ').val) θ = μ * (T θ).val)
  (capstan_integral : Real.log ((T θ_total).val / (T 0).val) = μ * θ_total)
  (theta_def : θ_total = 2 * Real.pi * n)
  :
  n = (1 / (2 * Real.pi * μ)) * Real.log (M.val / m.val) := by sorry
Statement:
import Mathlib
import Aesop
import Mathlib.Tactic
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SI
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SIDeriv
open BigOperators Real Nat Topology Filter Rat UnitsSystem SI
unseal Rat.add Rat.mul Rat.sub Rat.inv
theorem Ch2_Q1
  (M m : Mass)
  (μ : ℝ)
  (n : ℝ)
  (θ_total : ℝ
:=2 * Real.pi * n)
  (T : ℝ → Force)
  (hdif:Differentiable ℝ T)
  (h_pos : 0 < M.val ∧ 0 < m.val ∧ 0 < μ)
  (hM_gt_m : M.val > m.val)
  (T_light_def : T 0 = m * g)
  (T_heavy_def : T θ_total = M * g)
  (capstan_differential : ∀ θ : ℝ, deriv (fun θ' => (T θ').val) θ = μ * (T θ).val)
  (capstan_integral : Real.log ((T θ_total).val / (T 0).val) = μ * θ_total)
  (theta_def : θ_total = 2 * Real.pi * n)
  :
  n = (1 / (2 * Real.pi * μ)) * Real.log (M.val / m.val) := by sorry
Informal_statement: EXAMPLE Ch2_Q1 Q: A uniform, massless rope is wrapped around a fixed cylinder; its two ends hang vertically with a heavier bucket of mass \(M=1000\ \mathrm{kg}\) on one end and a lighter bucket of mass \(m=10\ \mathrm{kg}\) on the other. The coefficient of static friction between rope and cylinder is \(\mu=0.050\). Let \(n\) be the number of full turns the rope makes around the cylinder. Prove that the minimal number of turns required to keep the system in equilibrium is \[ n \;=\; \frac{1}{2\pi\mu}\,\ln\!\frac{M}{m}. \]

Name: competition_mechanics_Ch2_Q2
Header:
import Mathlib
import Aesop
import Mathlib.Tactic
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SI
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SIDeriv
open BigOperators Real Nat Topology Filter Rat UnitsSystem SI
unseal Rat.add Rat.mul Rat.sub Rat.inv
Theorem:
theorem Ch2_Q2
  (m : Mass)
  (L : Length)
  (α : ℝ)
  (ω : Scalar (-time_unit))
  (μ : ℝ)
  (N f : Force)
  (h_pos : 0 < m.val ∧ 0 < μ ∧ 0 < ω ∧ 0 < g)
  (h_cos : Real.cos α ≠ 0)
  (h_horiz : (Real.sin α • N) - (Real.cos α • f) = (ω ** 2) * m * (Real.cos α • L))
  (h_vert  : (Real.cos α • N) + (Real.sin α • f) = g * m)
  (f_def : f = Real.sin α • m * g - Real.cos α ^ 2 • m * ω **2 * L/1)
  (N_def : N = Real.cos α • m * g + (2 * Real.sin α * Real.cos α) • m * ω **2 * L/1)
  (fric_bound : ‖f.val‖ ≤ μ * ‖N.val‖)
  :
  ∀ (ε : ℤ), (ε = 1 ∨ ε = -1) →
    (f = ((ε : ℝ) • μ • N) →
      L = ((Real.sin α - (ε : ℝ) * μ * Real.cos α) • g) / (Real.cos α * (Real.cos α + (ε : ℝ) * μ * Real.sin α)) • ω**2) := by sorry
Statement:
import Mathlib
import Aesop
import Mathlib.Tactic
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SI
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SIDeriv
open BigOperators Real Nat Topology Filter Rat UnitsSystem SI
unseal Rat.add Rat.mul Rat.sub Rat.inv
theorem Ch2_Q2
  (m : Mass)
  (L : Length)
  (α : ℝ)
  (ω : Scalar (-time_unit))
  (μ : ℝ)
  (N f : Force)
  (h_pos : 0 < m.val ∧ 0 < μ ∧ 0 < ω ∧ 0 < g)
  (h_cos : Real.cos α ≠ 0)
  (h_horiz : (Real.sin α • N) - (Real.cos α • f) = (ω ** 2) * m * (Real.cos α • L))
  (h_vert  : (Real.cos α • N) + (Real.sin α • f) = g * m)
  (f_def : f = Real.sin α • m * g - Real.cos α ^ 2 • m * ω **2 * L/1)
  (N_def : N = Real.cos α • m * g + (2 * Real.sin α * Real.cos α) • m * ω **2 * L/1)
  (fric_bound : ‖f.val‖ ≤ μ * ‖N.val‖)
  :
  ∀ (ε : ℤ), (ε = 1 ∨ ε = -1) →
    (f = ((ε : ℝ) • μ • N) →
      L = ((Real.sin α - (ε : ℝ) * μ * Real.cos α) • g) / (Real.cos α * (Real.cos α + (ε : ℝ) * μ * Real.sin α)) • ω**2) := by sorry
Informal_statement: EXAMPLE Ch2_Q2 Q: A thin rod rotates about a vertical axis passing through its support with constant angular velocity \(\omega\). The rod makes an angle \(\alpha\) with the horizontal. A small ring of mass \(m\) can slide along the rod, and the coefficient of friction between the ring and the rod is \(\mu\). Prove that the stable positions \(L_1\) and \(L_2\) of the ring, measured along the rod from the axis of rotation, satisfy \[ L_{1} = \frac{g(\sin \alpha - \mu \cos \alpha)}{\omega^{2} \cos \alpha (\cos \alpha + \mu \sin \alpha)},\qquad L_{2} = \frac{g(\sin \alpha + \mu \cos \alpha)}{\omega^{2} \cos \alpha (\cos \alpha - \mu \sin \alpha)}. \]

Name: competition_mechanics_Ch2_Q3
Header:
import Mathlib

import Mathlib.Tactic
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SI

Theorem:
theorem Ch2_Q3
  (m : Mass)
  (R : Length)
  (θ : ℝ)
  (v : Speed)
  (μ : ℝ)
  (N f : Force)
  (h_pos : 0 < m.val ∧ 0 < μ ∧ 0 < g.val)
  (h_sin_cos : Real.sin θ ≠ 0 ∧ Real.cos θ ≠ 0)
  (r_def : r = Real.sin θ • R)
  (h_horiz : Real.sin θ • N - Real.cos θ • f = m * v**2/r)
  (h_vert  : Real.cos θ • N + Real.sin θ • f = m * g)
  (f_def : f = m * (Real.sin θ • g - Real.cos θ • v**2 / r /1))
  (N_def : N = m * (Real.cos θ • g + Real.sin θ • v**2 / r /1))
  (fric_bound : ‖f.val‖ ≤ μ * ‖N.val‖)
  :
  ∀ (ε : ℤ), (ε = 1 ∨ ε = -1) →
    (f = (ε : ℝ) • μ • N →
      v**2 = ((Real.sin θ - (ε : ℝ) * μ * Real.cos θ) • g * (Real.sin θ • R) / (Real.cos θ + (ε : ℝ) * μ * Real.sin θ)) ) := by sorry
Statement:

import Mathlib.Tactic

theorem Ch2_Q3
  (m : Mass)
  (R : Length)
  (θ : ℝ)
  (v : Speed)
  (μ : ℝ)
  (N f : Force)
  (h_pos : 0 < m.val ∧ 0 < μ ∧ 0 < g.val)
  (h_sin_cos : Real.sin θ ≠ 0 ∧ Real.cos θ ≠ 0)
  (r_def : r = Real.sin θ • R)
  (h_horiz : Real.sin θ • N - Real.cos θ • f = m * v**2/r)
  (h_vert  : Real.cos θ • N + Real.sin θ • f = m * g)
  (f_def : f = m * (Real.sin θ • g - Real.cos θ • v**2 / r /1))
  (N_def : N = m * (Real.cos θ • g + Real.sin θ • v**2 / r /1))
  (fric_bound : ‖f.val‖ ≤ μ * ‖N.val‖)
  :
  ∀ (ε : ℤ), (ε = 1 ∨ ε = -1) →
    (f = (ε : ℝ) • μ • N →
      v**2 = ((Real.sin θ - (ε : ℝ) * μ * Real.cos θ) • g * (Real.sin θ • R) / (Real.cos θ + (ε : ℝ) * μ * Real.sin θ)) ) := by sorry
Informal_statement: EXAMPLE Ch2_Q3 Q: A small ball of mass \(m\) moves inside a hollow sphere of radius \(R\), constrained to a horizontal circular path. The angle between the line connecting the ball to the sphere's center and the vertical is \(\theta\). The coefficient of static friction between the ball and the inner surface of the sphere is \(\mu\). Prove that the range of velocities \(v\) for which the ball can move stably is \[ v_{\min} = \sqrt{\frac{g (\sin\theta - \mu \cos\theta) R \sin\theta}{\cos\theta + \mu \sin\theta}}, \quad v_{\max} = \sqrt{\frac{g (\sin\theta + \mu \cos\theta) R \sin\theta}{\cos\theta - \mu \sin\theta}}. \]

Name: competition_mechanics_Ch2_Q4
Header:
import Mathlib
import Aesop
import Mathlib.Tactic
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SI
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SIDeriv
open BigOperators Real Nat Topology Filter Rat UnitsSystem SI
unseal Rat.add Rat.mul Rat.sub Rat.inv
Theorem:
theorem Ch2_Q4
  (m M : Mass)
  (θ : ℝ)
  (a_x a_y a_M : Acceleration)
  (N : Force)
  (h_pos : 0 < m.val ∧ 0 < M.val ∧ 0 < g)
  (h_sin : Real.sin θ ≠ 0)
  (h_cos : Real.cos θ ≠ 0)
  (h_block_x : m * a_x = - Real.sin θ • N)
  (h_block_y : m * a_y = Real.cos θ • N - m * g)
  (h_plane : M * a_M = Real.sin θ • N)
  (h_constraint : Real.sin θ • a_x - Real.cos θ • a_y =  Real.sin θ • a_M)
  :
  (a_x, a_y, a_M, N) =
    ( - ((Real.sin θ * Real.cos θ) • M) / (M + Real.sin θ ^2 • m) * g,
      - (Real.sin θ ^2 • (m + M)) / (M + Real.sin θ ^2 • m) * g,
      ((Real.sin θ * Real.cos θ) • m) / (M + Real.sin θ ^2 • m) * g,
      (Real.cos θ • m * M) / (M + Real.sin θ ^2 • m) * g ) := by sorry
Statement:
import Mathlib
import Aesop
import Mathlib.Tactic
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SI
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SIDeriv
open BigOperators Real Nat Topology Filter Rat UnitsSystem SI
unseal Rat.add Rat.mul Rat.sub Rat.inv
theorem Ch2_Q4
  (m M : Mass)
  (θ : ℝ)
  (a_x a_y a_M : Acceleration)
  (N : Force)
  (h_pos : 0 < m.val ∧ 0 < M.val ∧ 0 < g)
  (h_sin : Real.sin θ ≠ 0)
  (h_cos : Real.cos θ ≠ 0)
  (h_block_x : m * a_x = - Real.sin θ • N)
  (h_block_y : m * a_y = Real.cos θ • N - m * g)
  (h_plane : M * a_M = Real.sin θ • N)
  (h_constraint : Real.sin θ • a_x - Real.cos θ • a_y =  Real.sin θ • a_M)
  :
  (a_x, a_y, a_M, N) =
    ( - ((Real.sin θ * Real.cos θ) • M) / (M + Real.sin θ ^2 • m) * g,
      - (Real.sin θ ^2 • (m + M)) / (M + Real.sin θ ^2 • m) * g,
      ((Real.sin θ * Real.cos θ) • m) / (M + Real.sin θ ^2 • m) * g,
      (Real.cos θ • m * M) / (M + Real.sin θ ^2 • m) * g ) := by sorry
Informal_statement: EXAMPLE Ch2_Q4 Q: A block of mass $m$ rests on a frictionless inclined plane of mass $M$ and angle $\theta$. Both the block and the plane are free to move on a frictionless horizontal surface. Prove that the accelerations of the block $(a_x, a_y)$ and the plane $a_M$ with respect to the ground are \[ a_x = -\frac{M \sin \theta \cos \theta}{M + m \sin^2 \theta} g, \quad a_y = -\frac{(m+M) \sin^2 \theta}{M + m \sin^2 \theta} g, \quad a_M = \frac{m \sin \theta \cos \theta}{M + m \sin^2 \theta} g. \]

Name: competition_mechanics_Ch2_Q5
Header:
import Mathlib

unseal Rat.add Rat.mul Rat.sub Rat.inv
Theorem:
theorem Ch2_Q5
  (m : Mass)
  (R : Length)
  (h : Length)
  (θ : ℝ)
  (ω : Scalar (-time_unit))
  (N f : Force)
  (r : Length)
  (μ : ℝ)
  (h_pos : 0 < m.val ∧ 0 < R.val ∧ 0 < ω.val ∧ 0 < g)
  (h_height : h = Real.cos θ • R)
  (h_radius : r = Real.sin θ • R)
  (h_vert  : Real.sin θ • N + Real.cos θ • f = m * g)
  (h_horiz : Real.cos θ • N - Real.sin θ • f = m * ω ** 2 * r)
  (h_fric_min : ∀ μ', μ' < μ → ¬ ∃ (f : Force),
      |f| ≤ μ' • N ∧
      Real.sin θ • N + Real.cos θ • f = m * g ∧
      Real.cos θ • N - Real.sin θ • f = m * ω ** 2 * r)
  :
  μ = (Real.sin θ • (g - Real.cos θ • R * ω ** 2/1)).val / ( Real.cos θ • g /1 + (Real.sin θ) ^ 2 • R * ω ** 2).val := by sorry
Statement:

import Mathlib.Tactic

theorem Ch2_Q5
  (m : Mass)
  (R : Length)
  (h : Length)
  (θ : ℝ)
  (ω : Scalar (-time_unit))
  (N f : Force)
  (r : Length)
  (μ : ℝ)
  (h_pos : 0 < m.val ∧ 0 < R.val ∧ 0 < ω.val ∧ 0 < g)
  (h_height : h = Real.cos θ • R)
  (h_radius : r = Real.sin θ • R)
  (h_vert  : Real.sin θ • N + Real.cos θ • f = m * g)
  (h_horiz : Real.cos θ • N - Real.sin θ • f = m * ω ** 2 * r)
  (h_fric_min : ∀ μ', μ' < μ → ¬ ∃ (f : Force),
      |f| ≤ μ' • N ∧
      Real.sin θ • N + Real.cos θ • f = m * g ∧
      Real.cos θ • N - Real.sin θ • f = m * ω ** 2 * r)
  :
  μ = (Real.sin θ • (g - Real.cos θ • R * ω ** 2/1)).val / ( Real.cos θ • g /1 + (Real.sin θ) ^ 2 • R * ω ** 2).val := by sorry
Informal_statement: EXAMPLE Ch2_Q5 Q: A hollow spherical shell of radius R rotates around its vertical diameter with angular velocity ω. Inside the shell, at height h = R/2, a small block rotates together with the shell. Let μ be the coefficient of static friction between the block and the shell. Prove that the minimum μ required to maintain circular motion at height h is μ = ((g - R ω^2 cos θ) sin θ) / (g cos θ + R ω^2 sin^2 θ), where θ is the angle between the vertical and the line from the shell center to the block, and r = R sin θ.

Name: competition_mechanics_Ch3_Q1
Header:
import Mathlib
import Aesop
import Mathlib.Tactic
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SI
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SIDeriv
open BigOperators Real Nat Topology Filter Rat UnitsSystem SI
unseal Rat.add Rat.mul Rat.sub Rat.inv
Theorem:
theorem Ch3_Q1
  (k : Scalar (mass_unit - 2 • time_unit))
  (m1 m2 : Mass)
  (g : Acceleration)
  (x1 x2 : Length)
  (v_max : Speed)
  (h_pos : 0 < k.val ∧ 0 < m1.val ∧ 0 < m2.val ∧ 0 < g.val)
  (eq_x1 : x1 = (m1 + m2)/1 * g / k)
  (E_total : Energy
:=(1/2) • k * x1**2 - m1 * g * x1)
  (E_potential : Length → Energy
:=fun x => (1/2) • k * x**2 - m1 * g * x)
  (x2_def : (physderiv E_potential) x2 = 0)
  (v_max_def : (1/2) • m1 * v_max**2 = E_total - E_potential x2)
  :
  v_max**2 = (m2 * g)**2 / (m1 * k) := by sorry

  (m1 m2 : Mass)
  (g : Acceleration)
  (x1 x2 : Length)
  (v_max : Speed)
  (h_pos : 0 < k.val ∧ 0 < m1.val ∧ 0 < m2.val ∧ 0 < g.val)
  (eq_x1 : x1 = (m1 + m2)/1 * g / k)
  (E_total : Energy
:=(1/2) • k * x1**2 - m1 * g * x1)
  (E_potential : Length → Energy
:=fun x => (1/2) • k * x**2 - m1 * g * x)
  (x2_def : (physderiv E_potential) x2 = 0)
  (v_max_def : (1/2) • m1 * v_max**2 = E_total - E_potential x2)
  :
  v_max**2 = (m2 * g)**2 / (m1 * k) := by sorry
Informal_statement: EXAMPLE Ch3_Q1 Q: Two masses, $m_1$ and $m_2$, hang vertically from the lower end of a spring with stiffness $k$. After reaching equilibrium, $m_2$ is suddenly removed. Prove that the maximum velocity of $m_1$ after $m_2$ is removed is \[ v_{\max} = \frac{m_2 g}{\sqrt{m_1 k}}. \]

Name: competition_mechanics_Ch3_Q2
Header:
import Mathlib
import Aesop
import Mathlib.Tactic
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SI
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SIDeriv
open BigOperators Real Nat Topology Filter Rat UnitsSystem SI
unseal Rat.add Rat.mul Rat.sub Rat.inv
Theorem:
theorem Ch3_Q2
  (m : Mass)
  (k : Scalar (mass_unit - 2 • time_unit))
  (l0 : Length)
  (μ : ℝ)
  (x : Length)
  (E_spring : Length → Energy
:=fun x => (1/2) • (k * (x**2)))
  (W_friction : Length → Energy
:=fun Δx => μ • m * g * Δx)
  (E_total : Length → Energy
:=fun x => E_spring x - W_friction l0)
  (x_max_left : Length)
  (range_condition : μ • m * g * l0 ≤ (1/2) • k * l0**2)
  (single_cross_condition :(1/2) • k * l0**2 < μ • m * g * (l0 + 2 • x_max_left))
  (max_left_distance : μ •  m * g * (l0 + x_max_left) = (1/2) •  k * (l0**2 - x_max_left**2))
  :
  μ > (k * l0 / (4 • m * g)).val ∧ μ < (k * l0 / (2 • m * g)).val ∧ x_max_left ≤ l0 / 3 := by sorry
Statement:
import Mathlib
import Aesop
import Mathlib.Tactic
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SI
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SIDeriv
open BigOperators Real Nat Topology Filter Rat UnitsSystem SI
unseal Rat.add Rat.mul Rat.sub Rat.inv
theorem Ch3_Q2
  (m : Mass)
  (k : Scalar (mass_unit - 2 • time_unit))
  (l0 : Length)
  (μ : ℝ)
  (x : Length)
  (E_spring : Length → Energy
:=fun x => (1/2) • (k * (x**2)))
  (W_friction : Length → Energy
:=fun Δx => μ • m * g * Δx)
  (E_total : Length → Energy
:=fun x => E_spring x - W_friction l0)
  (x_max_left : Length)
  (range_condition : μ • m * g * l0 ≤ (1/2) • k * l0**2)
  (single_cross_condition :(1/2) • k * l0**2 < μ • m * g * (l0 + 2 • x_max_left))
  (max_left_distance : μ •  m * g * (l0 + x_max_left) = (1/2) •  k * (l0**2 - x_max_left**2))
  :
  μ > (k * l0 / (4 • m * g)).val ∧ μ < (k * l0 / (2 • m * g)).val ∧ x_max_left ≤ l0 / 3 := by sorry
Informal_statement: EXAMPLE Ch3_Q2 Q: A horizontal spring of stiffness k is fixed at one end and connected to a small ball of mass m at the other end. The spring is initially stretched to a distance l_0, and the coefficient of friction between the ball and the horizontal surface is μ. Prove that in order for the ball to pass through the equilibrium point O exactly once, μ must satisfy \frac{k l_0}{4 m g} < μ < \frac{k l_0}{2 m g}, and that under these conditions, the maximum distance l_1 the ball can travel past O to the left is l_1 ≤ \frac{l_0}{3}.

Name: competition_mechanics_Ch4_Q1
Header:
import Mathlib
import Aesop
import Mathlib.Tactic
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SI
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SIDeriv
open BigOperators Real Nat Topology Filter Rat UnitsSystem SI
unseal Rat.add Rat.mul Rat.sub Rat.inv
Theorem:
theorem Ch4_Q1
  (M m : Mass)
  (h_pos : 0 < M ∧ 0 < M ∧ 0 < G)
  (r : Time → Length)
  (phi : Time → ℝ)
  (F : Length → Force)
  (hF : ∀ (r₀ : Length), 0 < r₀ → (F r₀) = - (G * M * m) / (r₀ ** 2))
  (newton2 : ∀ t : Time,
      m.val * deriv (λ t' => deriv (λ t'' => (r (t'' • second)).val) t') t.val
      = (F (r t)).val)
  (L : Scalar (mass_unit + 2 • length_unit - time_unit))
  (hL : ∀ t : Time,
      (r t).val ^ 2 * deriv (λ t' => phi (t' • second)) t.val
      = L.val / m.val)
  (e : ℝ) (p : Length) (phi₀ : ℝ)
  (a : Length) (T : Time)
  :
  (∀ t : Time, (r t) = p / (1 + e * Real.cos (phi t - phi₀)))
  ∧ (∀ t : Time,
        deriv (λ t' => (1/2) * ((r (t' • second)).val ^ 2) * deriv (λ t'' => phi (t'' • second)) t') t.val
        = L.val / (2 * m.val))
  ∧ (T**2 = ((4 * (Real.pi)^2) • (a ** 3) / (G * M))) := by sorry
Statement:
import Mathlib
import Aesop
import Mathlib.Tactic
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SI
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SIDeriv
open BigOperators Real Nat Topology Filter Rat UnitsSystem SI
unseal Rat.add Rat.mul Rat.sub Rat.inv
theorem Ch4_Q1
  (M m : Mass)
  (h_pos : 0 < M ∧ 0 < M ∧ 0 < G)
  (r : Time → Length)
  (phi : Time → ℝ)
  (F : Length → Force)
  (hF : ∀ (r₀ : Length), 0 < r₀ → (F r₀) = - (G * M * m) / (r₀ ** 2))
  (newton2 : ∀ t : Time,
      m.val * deriv (λ t' => deriv (λ t'' => (r (t'' • second)).val) t') t.val
      = (F (r t)).val)
  (L : Scalar (mass_unit + 2 • length_unit - time_unit))
  (hL : ∀ t : Time,
      (r t).val ^ 2 * deriv (λ t' => phi (t' • second)) t.val
      = L.val / m.val)
  (e : ℝ) (p : Length) (phi₀ : ℝ)
  (a : Length) (T : Time)
  :
  (∀ t : Time, (r t) = p / (1 + e * Real.cos (phi t - phi₀)))
  ∧ (∀ t : Time,
        deriv (λ t' => (1/2) * ((r (t' • second)).val ^ 2) * deriv (λ t'' => phi (t'' • second)) t') t.val
        = L.val / (2 * m.val))
  ∧ (T**2 = ((4 * (Real.pi)^2) • (a ** 3) / (G * M))) := by sorry
Informal_statement: EXAMPLE Ch4_Q1 Q: A planet of mass m moves under the gravitational influence of the Sun of mass M, which is fixed at the origin. Using Newton's law of universal gravitation and Newton's second law of motion, prove Kepler's three laws of planetary motion: 1. The orbit of the planet is an ellipse with the Sun at one focus. 2. The radius vector joining the planet and the Sun sweeps out equal areas in equal times. 3. The square of the orbital period T is proportional to the cube of the semi-major axis a: T^2 ∝ a^3.

Name: competition_mechanics_Ch4_Q2
Header:
import Mathlib
import Aesop
import Mathlib.Tactic
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SI
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SIDeriv
open BigOperators Real Nat Topology Filter Rat UnitsSystem SI
unseal Rat.add Rat.mul Rat.sub Rat.inv
Theorem:
theorem Ch4_Q2
  (M m : Mass)
  (h_pos : 0 < M.val ∧ 0 < m.val)
  (p : Length) (e : ℝ)
  (r : Time → Length)
  (phi : Time → ℝ)
  (hd':Differentiable ℝ phi)
  (h_orbit : ∀ t : Time, r t = p / (1 + e * Real.cos (phi t)))
  (L : Scalar (mass_unit + 2 • length_unit - time_unit))
  (h_angmom : ∀ t : Time, (r t).val^2 * deriv (λ t' => phi (t' • second)) t.val = L.val / m.val)
  (a_r : Time → Acceleration)
  (hd:Differentiable ℝ a_r)
  (h_acc : ∀ t : Time,
    (a_r t).val =
      (deriv (λ t' => deriv (λ t'' => (r (t'' • second)).val) t') t.val)
      - (r t).val * (deriv (λ t' => phi (t' • second)) t.val)^2)
  (F_grav : Force
:=-((L ** 2) / ((m * p) * (r t) ** 2)))
  :
  ∀ t : Time, m * (a_r t) = F_grav := by sorry
Statement:

  (p : Length) (e : ℝ)
  (r : Time → Length)
  (phi : Time → ℝ)
  (hd':Differentiable ℝ phi)
  (h_orbit : ∀ t : Time, r t = p / (1 + e * Real.cos (phi t)))
  (L : Scalar (mass_unit + 2 • length_unit - time_unit))
  (h_angmom : ∀ t : Time, (r t).val^2 * deriv (λ t' => phi (t' • second)) t.val = L.val / m.val)
  (a_r : Time → Acceleration)
  (hd:Differentiable ℝ a_r)
  (h_acc : ∀ t : Time,
    (a_r t).val =
      (deriv (λ t' => deriv (λ t'' => (r (t'' • second)).val) t') t.val)
      - (r t).val * (deriv (λ t' => phi (t' • second)) t.val)^2)
  (F_grav : Force
:=-((L ** 2) / ((m * p) * (r t) ** 2)))
  :
  ∀ t : Time, m * (a_r t) = F_grav := by sorry
Informal_statement: EXAMPLE Ch4_Q2 Q: A planet moves in an ellipse about the Sun, with polar equation \( r(\varphi)=\dfrac{p}{1+e\cos\varphi} \). Prove that the central force required to produce this motion is proportional to \(1/r^2\); i.e. show \(F(r) = -C/r^2\) for some constant \(C\).

Name: competition_mechanics_Ch5_Q1
Header:
import Mathlib

Theorem:
theorem Ch5_Q1
  (m : Mass)
  (α : ℝ)
  (R : Length)
  (T : Force)
  (Δφ : ℝ)
  (Δl : Length)
  (Δm : Mass)
  (N : Force)
  (g : Acceleration)
  (h_pos : 0 < m.val ∧ 0 < g.val ∧ 0 < α ∧ α < Real.pi)
  (h_arc : Δl = Δφ • R)
  (h_mass : Δm = (m / ((2 * Real.pi) • R)) * Δl)
  (h_vert : Δm * g = Real.sin (α/2) • N)
  (h_horiz : (2 * Real.sin (Δφ/2)) • T = Real.cos (α/2) • N)
  (h_small : Real.sin (Δφ/2) = Δφ/2)
  (h_subst : Δm = (Δφ / (2 * Real.pi)) • m)
  :
  T = m * g / (2 * Real.pi * Real.tan (α/2)) := by sorry
Statement:

  (α : ℝ)
  (R : Length)
  (T : Force)
  (Δφ : ℝ)
  (Δl : Length)
  (Δm : Mass)
  (N : Force)
  (g : Acceleration)
  (h_pos : 0 < m.val ∧ 0 < g.val ∧ 0 < α ∧ α < Real.pi)
  (h_arc : Δl = Δφ • R)
  (h_mass : Δm = (m / ((2 * Real.pi) • R)) * Δl)
  (h_vert : Δm * g = Real.sin (α/2) • N)
  (h_horiz : (2 * Real.sin (Δφ/2)) • T = Real.cos (α/2) • N)
  (h_small : Real.sin (Δφ/2) = Δφ/2)
  (h_subst : Δm = (Δφ / (2 * Real.pi)) • m)
  :
  T = m * g / (2 * Real.pi * Real.tan (α/2)) := by sorry
Informal_statement: EXAMPLE Ch5_Q1 Q: A stationary right circular conical surface stands with its axis vertical and apex angle \(\alpha\). A uniform chain of total mass \(m\) is placed so that it rests in a horizontal circular loop around the cone (no friction between chain and cone). Prove that the tension \(T\) in the chain is \(\displaystyle T=\frac{m g}{2\pi}\cot\frac{\alpha}{2}=\frac{m g}{2\pi\tan\!\frac{\alpha}{2}}.\)

Name: competition_mechanics_Ch5_Q3
Header:
import Mathlib

unseal Rat.add Rat.mul Rat.sub Rat.inv
Theorem:
theorem Ch5_Q3
  (L : Length)
  (m : Mass)
  (g : Acceleration)
  (μ : ℝ)
  (θ : ℝ)
  (h : Length)
  (F : Force)
  (T N f : Force)
  (h_bounds : 0 ≤ h ∧ h ≤ L)
  (h_pos : 0 < L)
  (h_mass : 0 < m)
  (h_g : 0 < g)
  (h_mu_nonneg : 0 ≤ μ)
  (eq_horiz : F + f = Real.sin θ • T)
  (eq_vert : N + Real.cos θ • T = m * g)
  (eq_moment : Real.sin θ • T * L = F * h)
  (law_friction : f ≤ μ • N)
  : F ≤ (μ • (m * g) * (Real.tan θ • L)) / (Real.tan θ • (L - h) + μ • h) := by sorry
Statement:
import Mathlib
import Aesop
import Mathlib.Tactic
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SI
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SIDeriv
open BigOperators Real Nat Topology Filter Rat UnitsSystem SI
unseal Rat.add Rat.mul Rat.sub Rat.inv
theorem Ch5_Q3
  (L : Length)
  (m : Mass)
  (g : Acceleration)
  (μ : ℝ)
  (θ : ℝ)
  (h : Length)
  (F : Force)
  (T N f : Force)
  (h_bounds : 0 ≤ h ∧ h ≤ L)
  (h_pos : 0 < L)
  (h_mass : 0 < m)
  (h_g : 0 < g)
  (h_mu_nonneg : 0 ≤ μ)
  (eq_horiz : F + f = Real.sin θ • T)
  (eq_vert : N + Real.cos θ • T = m * g)
  (eq_moment : Real.sin θ • T * L = F * h)
  (law_friction : f ≤ μ • N)
  : F ≤ (μ • (m * g) * (Real.tan θ • L)) / (Real.tan θ • (L - h) + μ • h) := by sorry
Informal_statement: EXAMPLE Ch5_Q3 Q: A uniform thin rod of mass m and length L stands vertically on a rough horizontal floor; the coefficient of static friction at the foot is μ. The top end is held by a light inextensible rope making angle θ with the vertical rod. A horizontal force F (to the right) is applied at height h above the floor (0 ≤ h ≤ L). Prove the maximal F that prevents slipping.

  (m : Mass)

  (a : Acceleration)
  (l h : Length)
  (μ : Real)
  (f N1 N2 : Force)
  (assume_axles_on_ground : N1 ≥ 0 ∧ N2 ≥ 0)
  (assume_front_wheel_frictionless : true)
  (assume_rear_wheel_driven : true)
  (assume_car_rigid_body : true)
  (f_def : f = m * a)
  (vertical_force_balance : N1 + N2 = m * g)
  (moment_balance_about_G : f * h + N1 * l - N2 * l = 0)
  (static_friction_limit : f ≤ μ •  N2)
  : μ ≥ ((2 • l * a) / (g * l/1 + h * a)).val := by sorry

The distance between the front and rear wheels is $2l$, and the center of mass is located at the midpoint between the axles, at a height $h$ above the ground. The coefficient of friction between the rear wheels and the ground is $\mu$, while the front wheels are frictionless. Prove that the minimum value of $\mu$ required to prevent the rear wheels from slipping is \[ \mu \geq \frac{2 l a}{g l + h a}. \]

  (θ:Time → Dimensionless)
  (hθ:∀ t:Time,(θ t).val∈ Set.Ioo 0 (π/2))
  (x0 y0:Length)
  (hg:x0≥ 0∧ y0≥ 0)
  (hl:((2:ℝ) • l)**2=x0**2+y0**2)
  (hxy:∀ t:Time, (x t)**2+(y t)**2=x0**2+y0**2)
  (hxy0:x 0=x0 ∧ y 0=y0)
  (hI:I=((1/3:ℝ) • m*l**2))
  (hθ:∀ t:Time, Real.tan (θ t).val=(y t/x t).val)
  (hacc1:physderiv (physderiv x)=fun t=>(N1 t)/m)
  (hacc2:physderiv (physderiv y)=fun t=>(N2 t- m*g)/m)
  (hangacc:physderiv (physderiv θ)=fun t=> (l*( (Real.sin (θ t).val) • N1 t-(Real.cos (θ t).val) • N2 t)/I))
  (t0:Time)
  (ht0:t0>0)
  (ht0:N1 t0=0∧ ∀ t:Time, 0<t∧t<t0 → N1 t≠0)
  :y t0=(2/3:ℝ) • y0 := by sorry

Name: competition_mechanics_Ch7_Q1
Header:
import Mathlib

Theorem:
theorem Ch7_Q1
  (m : Mass)
  (k : Scalar (mass_unit - 2 • time_unit))
  (x0 : Length)
  (x_s : Time → Length)
  (ω : Scalar (-time_unit))
  (t_AB : Time)
  (h : Length)
  (l : Length)
  (v_B v1 v' : Speed)
  (x1 : Length)
  (x_comb : Time → Length)
  (h1:Differentiable ℝ x_comb)
  (ω' : Scalar (-time_unit))
  (A : Length) (φ' : ℝ)
  (g : Acceleration)
  (h_spring_motion : ∀ t : Time, x_s t =  Real.cos (ω.val * t.val) • x0)
  (h_spring_vel_at_collision : (physderiv x_s) t_AB = v1)
  (h_particle_energy : (1/2 : ℝ) •  m * v_B**2 = (m * g * h))
  (h_momentum : m * v1 + m * v_B = (2 •  m) * v')
  (h_omega_def : ω**2 = k / m)
  (h_omega'_def : ω'**2 = k / (2 • m))
  (h_pos_at_collision : x1 = x_s t_AB)
  (h_comb_pos0 : x_comb 0 = x1)
  (h_comb_vel0 : deriv (mathcast x_comb) (0:ℝ)  = v'.val)
  (h_comb_sho : ∀ t : Time, (deriv (fun t' => deriv (λ t'' => (x_comb (t'' • second)).val) t') t.val) + (ω'.val ^ 2) * (x_comb t).val = 0)
  :
  (∀ t : Time, x_comb t =  Real.cos (ω'.val * t.val + φ') • A) := by sorry

  (h_spring_vel_at_collision : (physderiv x_s) t_AB = v1)
  (h_particle_energy : (1/2 : ℝ) •  m * v_B**2 = (m * g * h))
  (h_momentum : m * v1 + m * v_B = (2 •  m) * v')
  (h_omega_def : ω**2 = k / m)

Informal_statement: EXAMPLE Ch7_Q1 Q: Two equal masses m: one attached to a spring (stiffness k) on a frictionless horizontal plane, the other slides down a smooth curve dropping by height h and reaches the spring at B at time t_AB. The spring was released from compression x0 at t=0 simultaneously with the sliding particle. At B a completely inelastic collision occurs; the masses stick together. Prove the post-collision motion is simple harmonic: x_comb(t) = A * cos (ω' t + φ'), with ω' = sqrt(k/(2m)) and A, φ' determined by the position and velocity at collision.

Name: competition_electromagnetism_Ch12_Q1
Header:

theorem Ch12_Q1
  (q1 q2 : Charge)
  (m1 m2 : Mass)
  (ε0 : Scalar (-mass_unit -3 • length_unit + 2 • time_unit + 2 • charge_unit))
  (r : Time → Length)
  (h:Differentiable ℝ r)
  (r0 : Length)
  (m : Mass
:=(m1 * m2) / (m1 + m2))
  (F_coul : Length → Force
:=fun r => (q1 * q2) / ((4 * Real.pi) • ε0 * r**2))
  (h_eq_motion : ∀ t : Time, (m.val * deriv (λ t' => deriv (λ τ => (r (τ • second)).val) t') t.val) = - (F_coul (r (t))).val)
  (h_init_rest : deriv (λ t' => (r (t' • second)).val) (0 : ℝ)  = 0)
  (h_init_sep : r 0 = r0)
  (h_opposite : (q1 * q2).val < 0)
  :
  ∃ t_coll : Time,
    r t_coll = 0 ∧
    t_coll.val = π * r0.val * Real.sqrt (((Real.pi • ε0 * r0 * m) / (-2 • q1 * q2)).val) := by sorry

:=fun r => (q1 * q2) / ((4 * Real.pi) • ε0 * r**2))

  (h_init_rest : deriv (λ t' => (r (t' • second)).val) (0 : ℝ)  = 0)

  (T0 T : Temperature)
  (A : Dimensionless)
  (hT : ∀ T' : Scalar temperature_unit, (V T')/ V0 = T' / T0)
  (hCT : T = T0 + t)
  (hIce : τ = 0 → t = 0)
  (hBoil : τ = 100 • celsius → t = 100 • celsius)
  (hVpos : 0 < V0.val ∧ ∀T':Temperature, 0 < (V T').val)
  (hTpos : 0 < T0.val ∧ 0 < T.val)
  (hApos : 0 < A.val)
  (dV : Scalar (3 • length_unit))
  :

Informal_statement: EXAMPLE Ch8_Q1 Q: Dalton proposed a constant-pressure temperature scale. Under this scale, for a constant pressure, the relative increase of the ideal gas volume is proportional to the increase of Dalton temperature $\tau$, with the ice point defined at $\tau = 0^\circ$ and the boiling point at $\tau = 100^\circ$. Prove that the Dalton temperature $\tau$ can be expressed in terms of the Celsius temperature $t$ as \[ \tau = \frac{100 \ln \left(1 + \frac{t}{273.15}\right)}{\ln \frac{373.15}{273.15}}. \]

  (T : Time → Temperature)

  (h2:Differentiable ℝ ΔQ)
  (M : Scalar mass_unit)
  (c : Scalar (energy_unit - (mass_unit + temperature_unit)))
  (cycle_time : Time)

  (work_done : Energy)
  (first_law : ∀ t:Time, deriv (λ t' => (ΔQ (t' • second)).val) t.val = M.val * c.val * deriv (λ t' => (T (t' • second)).val) t.val )
  :
  ¬ (∃ c_const : Scalar (energy_unit - (mass_unit + temperature_unit)),
       ∀ t : Time, (physderiv ΔQ) t = c_const*  M * ((physderiv T) t)) := by sorry

Let the system return to its initial state after completing the cycle. Prove that the specific heat capacity \(c\) of the gas cannot be a constant throughout the cyclic process.

Name: competition_thermodynamics_Ch9_Q2
Header:

Theorem:
theorem Ch9_Q2
  (M : Mass)
  (μ : Scalar (mass_unit - amount_unit))
  (R : Scalar (energy_unit - temperature_unit - amount_unit))
  (cV : Scalar (energy_unit - temperature_unit - mass_unit))
  (p0 : Pressure)
  (T0 : Temperature)
  (p : Pressure)
  (V : Scalar (3 • length_unit))
  (T : Temperature)
  (ideal_gas_law : p * V = (M / μ) * R * T)
  (shifted_isotherm : (p - p0) * V = (M / μ) * R * T0)
  (ΔV : Scalar (3 • length_unit))
  (ΔT : Temperature)
  (ΔU : Energy)
  (ΔQ : Energy)
  (first_law : ΔQ = ΔU + p * ΔV/1)
  (internal_energy : ΔU = cV * M * ΔT)
  :
  ∃ c : Scalar (energy_unit - temperature_unit - mass_unit),
    c = cV/1 + (R / (μ * p0)) * p := by sorry
Statement:

theorem Ch9_Q2
  (M : Mass)
  (μ : Scalar (mass_unit - amount_unit))
  (R : Scalar (energy_unit - temperature_unit - amount_unit))
  (cV : Scalar (energy_unit - temperature_unit - mass_unit))
  (p0 : Pressure)
  (T0 : Temperature)
  (p : Pressure)
  (V : Scalar (3 • length_unit))
  (T : Temperature)
  (ideal_gas_law : p * V = (M / μ) * R * T)
  (shifted_isotherm : (p - p0) * V = (M / μ) * R * T0)
  (ΔV : Scalar (3 • length_unit))
  (ΔT : Temperature)
  (ΔU : Energy)
  (ΔQ : Energy)
  (first_law : ΔQ = ΔU + p * ΔV/1)
  (internal_energy : ΔU = cV * M * ΔT)
  :
  ∃ c : Scalar (energy_unit - temperature_unit - mass_unit),
    c = cV/1 + (R / (μ * p0)) * p := by sorry
Informal_statement: EXAMPLE Ch9_Q2 Q: An ideal gas with molar mass \mu and constant-volume heat capacity c_V undergoes a process x. The process x can be obtained by shifting a T_0-isothermal curve downward by p_0 on the p-V plane. Prove that, at any point of process x, the heat capacity c satisfies c = c_V + \frac{R}{\mu p_0} p, where p is the gas pressure at that point.
